question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any funding that has been made available to women’s groups in North Down as part of the Communities in Transition programme.
answer_text: Communities in Transition (CIT) does not currently fund any Women’s Groups in North Down.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how her Department’s new road maintenance strategy will differ from the Department’s previous road maintenance strategy.
answer_text: The road network is a critical element of infrastructure that underpins and supports our society and economy well-being. It is used either directly or in-directly by almost everyone on a daily basis. We all know the challenges that we have seen in terms of keeping that network in good condition and I know from my constituency role how important it is to the people we represent. The well-discussed funding shortfall, and the years of Limited Service, have seen the condition of our roads deteriorate and its time we tried something different. That’s why a new approach to road maintenance is one of my Foundations.We need a new approach to how the road network is maintained, one that focuses our limited resources in a way that balances safety and value for money. It should be data driven and supported by sound engineering judgement and address the problem in a meaningful and sustainable manner.My officials are working on a new Road Maintenance Strategy that I will be considering in detail in the coming weeks. We will focus on delivering higher-quality repairs instead of spreading resources too thinly, ensuring a more reliable and safer road network where we intervene.We will use modern technology to direct our investments where they are needed most, ensuring smarter maintenance decisions and will future proof our roads to meet the changing needs of our society including active travel, reducing the carbon footprint of our operations, and ensuring that the network is resilient after adverse weather events.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of missed appointments in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust in each of the last three years; and (ii) how many of these were caused by postal issues.
answer_text: The number of appointments not attended in the South Eastern HSC Trust in each of the last three years is indicated below. This is for consultant-led, face to face outpatient appointments, both new and review. The data relates to the number of patients who did not attend (DNA) their appointment and did not inform the hospital, or informed them on the day the appointment was scheduled.YearHSC TrustNew Outpatients Review Outpatients2021/22South Eastern5,83513,3282022/23South Eastern6,35914,1892023/24South Eastern            5,689           12,779 Source: DoH Outpatient Activity datasetIn terms of providing data on how many of these missed appointments were due to postal reasons, unfortunately the reasons for patients not attending their appointments are not known and therefore can not be recorded. To ascertain the reasons for non-attendance would require each Trust to undertake retrospective audits of those patients who failed to attend their appointment. Given the resources required to undertake this work, only small-scale audits have been undertaken by Trusts to date.My Department has recently agreed to undertake an audit to ascertain the reasons why patients did not attend their appointment or treatment. The initial audit will focus on the South Eastern HSC Trust and should be completed by 31 March 2025. Pending the outcome of the audit, changes may be made to the current processes for engaging with patients, including booking letters and text reminders.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department’s new road maintenance strategy will include the repair of potholes less than 50mm in depth.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.As a result of these budget constraints, my Department’s road maintenance standards have been reduced to a limited service, so that only the highest priority defects across the network are being repaired. This means defects greater than 50mm depth are currently being repaired across all roads, including low trafficked rural roads. On high traffic urban and rural roads, defects greater than 20mm are also being repaired. The level of repairs that can be carried out in future financial years will depend on the 2025-26 final budget determination. Until such times as sufficient funding is made available, it will not be possible to change the current level of service. However, my officials are working on a new Road Maintenance Strategy that I will be considering in detail in the coming weeks. We will focus on delivering higher-quality repairs instead of spreading resources too thinly, ensuring a more reliable and safer road network where we intervene.We will use modern technology to direct our investments where they are needed most, ensuring smarter maintenance decisions and will future proof our roads to meet the changing needs of our society including active travel, reducing the carbon footprint of our operations, and ensuring that the network is resilient after adverse weather events.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the services not currently available due to vacancies at (i) HMP Maghaberry; (ii) HMP Magilligan; and (iii) HMP Hydebank Wood.
answer_text: There are no services currently unavailable at prison establishments as a result of prison officer vacancies.  However, it is inevitable that the continued rise in the prison population is placing all services within prisons under pressure.  The Governor of each prison makes adjustments to the establishment regime and deploys available staff to ensure that the most effective and consistent delivery of services can be maintained.I also previously approved an increase in prison officer numbers.  Those additional staff will commence training within the next few weeks and a further recruitment exercise is planned for later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action her Department has taken in relation to issues raised by Retail NI, the Federation of Small Businesses and other retail lobby groups regarding the challenges faced by high street businesses as a result of changes to employers' National Insurance contributions and other running costs.
answer_text: National Insurance contributions are set by the British Government. The Executive does not have the financial capacity to compensate for decisions made on reserved taxation matters. Nevertheless, I’m aware that local high street businesses have raised concerns about the impact of increased labour costs as a result of the British Government’s Autumn Statement. These concerns are being examined as part of Cost of Doing Business research which I commissioned as Finance Minister and which is due to be completed later this month. It will help build the evidence base for making the case for the Treasury to act on the cost of doing business here.My colleague, the Minister of Finance, highlighted the adverse impacts of the taxation system with the Chief Secretary to the Treasury at the Finance Interministerial Standing Committee on 27 February. I will work to support him and advocate alongside him where possible. We will continue to engage with businesses to help us in making persuasive representations to Treasury.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail her Department’s spending on interpreter services in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The amount spent by my Department on interpreter services has not been recorded by my Department in a way that makes it possible to identify the relevant costs in the specific format requested.The table below outlines expenditure on interpreter costs for the last five financial years from 2019-20 to 2023-24. However, some costs cannot be shown separately from translation costs due to the way they are recorded.Financial YearInterpreter CostsInterpreter & Translation & Costs that cannot be separately identifiedTotal Costs£k£k£k2019-203062095152020-211891102992021-223961785742022-235061476532023-245627569

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the expected timescale for the completion of the Project Gigabit procurement phase.
answer_text: The procurement for Project Gigabit commenced on 11 November 2024, with an invitation to interested suppliers to complete a selection questionnaire to demonstrate their capacity and capability to deliver such a contract.  Those suppliers who meet the published criteria of the selection questionnaire will be invited to submit a response to the requirements of the Invitation to Tender for the project. The procurement will seek a supplier to address an initial scope of approximately 11,000 premises. My Department expects to award a contract for Project Gigabit in the coming months, subject to compliant bids being made, bids meeting the published criteria, and all necessary approvals being agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will prioritise the installation of LED lights in areas where street lights are constantly lit due to faults.
answer_text: Although my Department does not incur additional energy charges for street lights that are burning during the day, I can assure the member that I recognise, from an environmental perspective, that it is important that these are rectified as soon as possible.In some instances, particularly in rural areas, upgrading works by Northern Ireland Electricity on their supply network, can result in the removal of the overhead switch wire controlling the lighting.  Where this is the case, in order to keep the lights working at night-time, it may be necessary to leave them burning by day until new LED lights with individual photo electric cells can be installed.This work is carried out at the earliest opportunity, although there can be some delays due to the delivery times of the LED units.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail her Department’s spending on the Ending the Harm communications campaign.
answer_text: For the period between April 2024 and March 2025, £185,000 has been spent on the current Ending the Harm campaign, which was launched in October 2024. This ‘statement’ creative was initially developed as part of a previous Ending the Harm campaign, which has been adapted for this new campaign. Therefore, the campaign cost less than £5,000 to develop.  The campaign gives a name to the many ways that paramilitaries exploit vulnerable people; and it helps to expose and challenge the true nature of paramilitary influence so that it cannot hide in plain sight. It has appeared in communities across Northern Ireland and is also present on bus advertising and social media, providing further visibility in geographical areas where there may be a lack of advertising space. You can view the posters on the EPPOC website: endingtheharm.com.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any support measures her Department plans to introduce for high street businesses that will be impacted by the changes to employers' National Insurance contributions.
answer_text: National Insurance contributions are set by the British Government. The Executive does not have the financial capacity to compensate for decisions made on reserved taxation matters.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 22448/22-27, to detail the current prioritised list of flood alleviation schemes within DfI Rivers Directorate capital works programme.
answer_text: My Departments Rivers Directorate has a 10 year capital works programme to plan investment in both new flood defences and existing drainage infrastructure. Projects can be identified from the regional Flood Risk Management Plan where we have used predictive flood modelling to identify locations that are at most risk of flooding In addition projects can be identified through our local knowledge of places that commonly flood or have a history of flooding. Projects are prioritised based on a range of criteria including current level of flood risk, estimated cost of flood alleviation works and the benefit to cost ratio of potential works. Currently we have over 50 separate projects on the programme, with approximately half of these in progress at various different stages. You can find more information on each of these projects on our website including an interactive map detailing each scheme: DfI Rivers - Flood Alleviation Schemes

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement his Department has had with the UK Government in relation to the review of physician and anaesthesia associates.
answer_text: An independent review of Physician Associates and Anaesthesia Associates was launched by the UK Health and Social Care Secretary Wes Streeting on 20 November 2024. While the remit of the review is limited to England only, the Review Team are actively engaging with the Devolved Administrations, including the provision of weekly progress updates to my officials.My Department is currently undertaking a separate evaluation of the Physician Associate role across the HSC.  This will take into account any relevant outcomes from the review in England.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of missed appointments in the South Eastern Health and Social Trust during 2024.
answer_text: The number of appointments not attended in the South Eastern HSC Trust in 2023/24 is indicated below. This is for consultant-led, face to face outpatient appointments, both new and review. The data relates to the number of patients who did not attend (DNA) their appointment and did not inform the hospital, or informed them on the day the appointment was scheduled.  YearHSC TrustNew Outpatients Review Outpatients2023/24South Eastern            5,689           12,779 Source: DoH Outpatient Activity datasetThe high number of missed appointments is unacceptable. These are wasted appointment slots which prevent other patients being seen and therefore lead to longer waiting times.To help better understand the reason for non-attendance, my Department has agreed to undertake a retrospective audit to ascertain the reasons why patients did not attend their appointment or treatment. The initial audit will focus on the South Eastern HSC Trust and should be completed by 31 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement his Department has had with the UK Government in relation to the potential reclassification of ketamine as a Class A drug.
answer_text: The policy and legal frameworks relating to the use of drugs, including the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 and the Psychoactive Substances Act 2016, are reserved to the UK Government and any change to the current classification of controlled drugs would be the responsibility of the Home Office, based on expert advice from the Advisory Council on the Misuse of Drugs (ACMD). However, the Northern Ireland Justice and Health Departments work closely with the other devolved nations and the UK Government on substance use related issues. The Minister of Justice and I participated in a recent UK Drugs Ministerial meeting chaired by the UK Government where the potential re-classification of Ketamine was discussed in advance of the upcoming report from the ACMD into ketamine and its associated harms. I have also written a joint letter with Minister Long to the UK Home Office Minister for Policing, Fire and Crime Prevention to welcome the UK Government’s decision to commission expert advice on reviewing the classification of this dangerous substance, and asked the Home Office to ensure that arrangements are put in place to allow affected families and relevant stakeholders from Northern Ireland to inform this independent review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any additional measures to address the spread of bovine TB introduced following the publication of the Chief Veterinary Officer Review of Bovine Tuberculosis in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: In April 2024, I asked Brian Dooher, Chief Veterinary Officer (CVO), to carry out a comprehensive review of bovine tuberculosis (bTB) in Northern Ireland. Following extensive engagement with stakeholders, scientists and neighbouring jurisdictions, this review was completed and published in November 2024.  It considered the actions in the 2022 Bovine TB Eradication Strategy along with setting out a number of new proposals brought forward by the CVO.		I immediately agreed with one of the CVO’s recommendations to establish a new bTB Partnership Steering Group (TBPSG), to work with officials in the development of a new delivery plan.  This group was established in January with several meetings having already taken place, the most recent engagement occurring on 26 February 2025.  The group is, firstly, focusing on identifying the immediate priorities for action within the review and the steps needed to put them in place.  I have asked that officials report to me on the draft delivery plan by the end of March 2025.		It is my intention that the delivery plan, which will draw upon the Chief Veterinary Officer’s proposals alongside the actions within the 2022 bTB Eradication Strategy for Northern Ireland, will clearly set out our priorities to be taken forward in the time ahead to tackle the unacceptable levels of bTB in Northern Ireland.	In addition, I have also written to Ministerial colleagues across the UK and Ireland to seek their support for the CVO’s proposal to enhance CVO North-South and East-West co-operation on bTB.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 22446/22-27, to detail the number of individuals, who received an out-of-court diversion, who have subsequently been convicted for possession of a Class B drug in each of the last five years.
answer_text: In the period 2019 – 2023, the most recent five-year period for which information is available, 3,595 people received an out of court diversionary disposal for a possession of class B drugs. Of those individuals, up to the end of 2023, 695 had a subsequent conviction for an offence in relation to Possession of Class B drugs.Note:Figures for convictions relate to initial disposal at court only.  Results of appeals are not included. Diversionary disposals include cautions, informed warnings and Youth Conference Plans.  Cases dealt with by Community Resolution Notice are not included.Figures included in AQW 22446 22-27 included the total number of convictions, whereas the answer in this question provides data on individual defendants who were had received at least one diversion for the offence specified and who had a subsequent conviction at court for at least one offence related to Possession of Class B drugs.  Some people may have had more than one diversion or conviction for the relevant offences during the period examined.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 22545/22-27, to detail the time elapsed since her Department first began assessing each of the five remaining individuals’ cases.
answer_text: The assessment of the convictions of the five remaining individuals commenced on the following dates:11 November 2024 – one individual.13 December 2024 – three individuals.12 February 2025 – one individual.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 22446/22-27, to detail the number of individuals who, after serving a custodial sentence, have subsequently been convicted for possession of a Class B drug in each of the last five years.
answer_text: In the period 2019 – 2023, the most recent five year period for which information is available, 879 people received a custodial sentence following conviction at court for a possession of class B drugs offence. Of those individuals, up to the end of 2023, 314 had a subsequent conviction for an offence in relation to Possession of Class B drugs.Note:Figures for convictions relate to initial disposal at court only.  Results of appeals are not included. Figures included in AQW 22446 22-27 included the total number of convictions, whereas the answer in this question provides data on individual defendants who had a custodial sentence imposed in the period 2019 – 2023 for an offence related to Possession of Class B drugs and who then had a further conviction for at least one offence related to Possession of Class B drugs, whether or not that conviction resulted in a custodial disposal. Some individuals may have had more than one conviction for the relevant offences during the period examined.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 22448/22-27, to detail the expected timescale for the conclusion of the feasibility study.
answer_text: The feasibility study for Bangor has not yet been commissioned and therefore no programme for the study is currently available. The Bangor feasibility study is to assess the river and tidal flood risk to properties within the area and to identify if there are any economically viable schemes to reduce the impacts of river and/or coastal flooding. However, I can advise that a study of this nature typically takes at least 18 months to complete. It is currently anticipated that this study will commence in summer 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail a timescale for the reopening of Cairn Wood.
answer_text: My Department’s Forest Service has a partnership arrangement in place with Ards and North Down Brough Council, whereby Council has responsibility for the management and provision of recreational facilities in Cairn Wood, Ballysallagh forest. This also includes decisions around reinstating public access to Cairn Wood.The Council has reported that Storm Éowyn caused extensive damage to this forest and therefore remains closed for public safety reasons. Forest Service staff have engaged with council officials who have advised that works are scheduled, to enable the re-opening of the site, and further public notification will be issued when these are complete. It is anticipated that Cairn Wood recreational facilities will be gradually reopened over the next 2-3 weeks
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to providing a certificate of exemption from the Taxi Licencing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 to wedding car operators.
answer_text: Following receipt of correspondence from the wedding car industry in 2021, regarding the use of exemption certificates under the Taxis Act (NI) 2008, this matter was fully considered.On 21 December 2021, the wedding car industry was advised that after giving due cognizance to arguments made by the industry and legal advice obtained on the matter, that the Department would not allow the use of exemption certificates. The decision was taken after carefully considering the effect of such an exemption in terms of public road safety given this is the main purpose of regulating those who drive for hire and reward, including the wedding car industry.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of properties that will benefit from Project Gigabit in the North Down constituency, broken down by postcode.
answer_text: There are 68 premises in the North Down constituency in the target intervention area for Project Gigabit. Because Project Gigabit is still at the procurement phase, I am unable to provide the details on the premises that will benefit. These premises will only be known once the procurement is completed and if a supplier is appointed. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to amending the Taxi Licencing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 to exempt wedding cars.
answer_text: Wedding cars are currently required to comply with the Taxi Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015, as they are vehicles which carry passengers for hire or reward in the course of their business. My Officials have recently provided me with options for reviewing the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 and associated Regulations.  I am currently considering those options.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the rationale behind the decision not to award funding from the Core Grant Funding Scheme 2025-26 to the Samaritans.
answer_text: I refer the member to my response to AQW 22710/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on her Department’s replacement of street lighting with LED lights.
answer_text: My Department’s programme to replace its sodium street lights with more energy efficient LED lighting units has been in place since 2016 and is now well advanced.  To date 238,950 LED lighting units have been installed representing approximately 82% of the overall street lighting network.  My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for several years due to underfunding. As a result, the capital budget for my Department in 2024/25 has been less than required and available funding must be prioritised accordingly.  Unfortunately, this means that there has been limited progress on this programme during the current financial year.I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure that appropriate investment is made to allow this replacement programme to be completed, however the timescales for replacing the remaining sodium street lights with LED units will depend on future funding being made available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will consider expanding water quality sampling outside of the bathing season at Ballyholme Beach.
answer_text: My department recognises that there has been a marked increase in open water swimming with a considerable number of people accessing bathing waters all year.  Responses to the latest Bathing Water Review indicated overwhelming public support for an extension to the season. Given the significant policy change, it is important that all implications are considered.  This will take time, resources and further public consultation.  In this context, my department has begun the process of sampling outside of the bathing season to inform future policy direction. This data is published on the department’s website.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the implementation of a baby loss certificate scheme.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer for AQW 22758-22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of vehicle damage claims where her Department agreed to pay compensation in 2024; and (ii) the value of these claims.
answer_text: My Department maintains its records by financial year rather than by calendar year.  The most recent management information held in relation to roads related public liability claims can be found online https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-asset-maintenance-directorate-management-information-roads-related-claims-period-20192020 Table 2 in the above link provides details of (i) the number of vehicle damage claims where my Department agreed to pay compensation.Table 7.1 in the above link provides details of (ii) the value of these claims.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any additional financial support he intends to provide to community pharmacies in light of incoming changes to employers' National Insurance contributions.
answer_text: Following the announcement of the 2025-26 Draft Budget allocation my department has assessed that we will be facing a significant funding gap in 2025-26 as a result of both inflationary pressures and increased demand.However, in recognition of the valuable role played by community pharmacy and other providers affected, I will be considering the funding pressures associated with the changes to employer’s National Insurance contributions alongside other priorities before I make any final funding decisions

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 21455/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether he is considering a proposal from the Interim Public Sector Transformation Board to alleviate pressures on the Planning Appeals Commission.
answer_text: I will formally announce the successful projects to be funded through transformation funding in the near future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current average waiting time for an endometriosis diagnosis, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The information requested is not routinely collated and could only be made available at a disproportionate cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any action his Department is taking to address prolonged waiting times for endometriosis treatments.
answer_text: As you may be aware, my Department has recently published the Getting It Right First Time (GIRFT) Report for Gynaecology. The report identifies 20 general recommendations to improve gynaecology services through coordinated regional action, as well as a list of specific recommendations for each Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust to take forward. These recommendations have been accepted in principle with a view to implementing the required improvements as soon as it is feasible; however, it is acknowledged that some recommendations may require additional funding and lead-in time to deliver.My officials have established a regional forum, with Trust, primary care and Public Health Agency (PHA) representation, to take forward these recommendations.  It is also planned that a regional engagement forum will also be set up to garner expert user feedback and ensure that patient’s voices are heard. My Department recognises reform and improvement in the delivery of Endometriosis services are a priority and a task and finish group has been formed to focus on this service area. You will be aware that the Belfast and Western HSC Trusts previously held provisional accreditation as a British Society of Gynaecological Endoscopy (BSGE) Endometriosis Centre and this accreditation was impacted on during Covid. Part of this group’s remit will be to determine how quickly accreditation can be achieved again in Northern Ireland, while recognising the need to balance theatre capacity for urgent or cancer surgeries, acknowledging our funding constraints.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the status of the current local development plans, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: All councils are currently preparing their new Local Development Plans (LDPs) to provide the spatial framework for development for their areas over a 15-year period.To date four councils have now adopted their Plan Strategies (PS), and two further councils have received the Department’s direction to adopt their PS with modifications (Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council (ANBC) and Derry City and Strabane District Council (DCSDC)).  All Council LDP’s progress is summarised below:Council timetables indicate that three will publish their draft Plan Strategies (dPS) later this year.  Officials continue to engage with those councils preparing for the publication of their dPS and draft Local Policy Plans (dLPP).    ANBCANDBCABCBCCCCGBCDCSDCFODCLCCCMEABCMUDCNMDDCStatement of community involvementTimetablePreferred options paper (POP)Plan Strategy (PS) PublishedSubmitted to Department to cause the IEIE causedIndependentexaminationPAC Report with Dept.Binding Direction issued to councilAdoption of PSI am, of course aware that in recent times there have been increasing concerns about both the length of time LDPs are taking to adopt and the resourcing issues that exist within the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC). Whilst the power to use an appointed person exists to conduct an Independent Examination (IE) it has not been utilised as the PAC are the specified body set up in legislation to deal with such matters. However, I am committed to ensuring that our planning system operates in an efficient and effective way and you will be aware that my Department recently submitted a bid to the Interim Public Sector Transformation Board. The Department’s bid included seeking funding for independent examiners for Local Development Plan Independent Examinations and for Public Local Inquiries and Hearings for Departmental planning applications. This would allow us to help address the current backlogs in such work and allow us to consider new approaches to commissioning this work in the future.I am awaiting confirmation from the Board if the bid has been successful.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of vacancies within the private streets staffing structure in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. This has had a significant impact on my Department’s ability to deliver essential services, and to fill vacant posts. The number of Full Time Equivalent (FTE) vacancies within the Private Streets function is shown in the table below: 2020-212021-222022-232023-242024-25Private Street Vacancies5.46.45.47.46.5

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of convictions for using a mobile phone while driving in 2024.
answer_text: Provisional figures for 2024 indicate that there were 219 cases dealt with at courts where there was a conviction for an offence of using a mobile phone while driving.  The number of convictions for cases dealt with in 2023, the most recent year for which official statistics are available, was 324.Note:1. Figures for convictions relate to initial disposal at court only.  Results of appeals are not included. 2. Figures relate to defendants who were convicted at court, for at least one offence related to using a mobile phone while driving.3. Figures for 2024 are based on management information and should be considered provisional.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of convictions for drink and drug driving related offences in 2024.
answer_text: Provisional figures for 2024 indicate that there were 2,403 cases dealt with at courts where there was a conviction for a drink or drug driving offence.  The number of convictions for cases dealt with in 2023, the most recent year for which official statistics are available, was 2,478.Note:1. Figures for convictions relate to initial disposal at court only.  Results of appeals are not included. 2. Figures relate to defendants who were convicted at court, for at least one drink or drug driving related offence.3. Figures for 2024 are based on management information and should be considered provisional.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) the number of letters issued by her Department to sub-postmasters and sub-postmistresses in relation to the quashing of convictions; and (ii) the number of letters still to be issued.
answer_text: To date, my Department has issued letters to sixteen individuals whose convictions have been quashed by the Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Act 2024 (‘the Act’).The convictions of five remaining individuals are currently being assessed, and I am hopeful that more letters can be issued shortly.  I would also encourage anyone who believes that their convictions may be within scope of the Act to contact my Department, so that I can be assured that all relevant convictions have been identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on flood alleviation measures in Bangor, as outlined in the Second Cycle Northern Ireland Flood Risk Management Plan 2021-27.
answer_text: Bangor has been identified as one of 12 Areas of potential significant flood risk and as a result is included on my Rivers Directorate Capital Works Programme.All potential schemes on the Capital Works Programme are prioritised based on a number of criteria including, any existing level of flood protection, benefit to cost ratio and estimated delivery cost. Due to its position relative to other schemes on this prioritised list, the feasibility study for this area has not yet commenced. However, it is currently anticipated that the Bangor feasibility study will commence in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the impact that delays to planned upgrades to Kinnegar Wastewater Treatment Works will have on water quality in Belfast Lough.
answer_text: I am concerned at the delays to planned upgrades to Kinnegar Wastewater Treatment Works.  Belfast Lough is already failing to meet ‘Good’ status and a Shellfish Water Protected Area in the inner Lough is failing to meet water quality objectives with evidence of a deteriorating situation at some shellfish sites.  		The four identified bathing waters in this water body are at Helen’s Bay, Crawfordsburn, Ballyholme and Groomsport, with an additional candidate site at Brompton.  Although all of these sites are currently passing the standards, there is no room for complacency. 		A significant pressure within the Belfast Lough catchment is from wastewater. The programme of measures within the River Basin Management Plan to improve water quality in Belfast Lough is reliant on appropriate investment in wastewater infrastructure. 		I will also shortly be bringing the Third River Basin Management Plan to the Executive with my proposals to improve water quality.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an updated timescale for the planned upgrades to Kinnegar Wastewater Treatment Works.
answer_text: The proposed upgrade of Kinnegar Wastewater Treatment Works (WwTW) is included in the Living With Water in Belfast Plan which, having been endorsed by the NI Executive, was published by my Department in November 2021. Following an increase in the estimated cost of the NI Water elements of the Plan pushed the overall costs from £1.4 to £2.1 billion, my Department conducted an affordability review of the Plan. This review concluded that the need for the Plan continues to exist, however, the original timescale of 12 years is no longer achievable. Delivery of the projects in the Belfast Plan, including Kinnegar WwTW, will now be taken forward by my Department and NI Water as normal business at a scale and pace achievable within available budgets.  NI Water has subsequently advised that delivery of this project is now being paused. However, NI Water is continuing to progress the planning application, to ensure that if additional capital funding becomes available, it will be in a position to proceed with delivery.   NI Water has further advised that, to ensure that the existing facility operates as effectively as possible until it has been upgraded, it will commence a programme of maintenance in Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any engagement her Department has had with the UK Government in relation to the change of classification of ketamine to a Class A drug.
answer_text: Recently both the Minister of Health and I attended a meeting with Ministers from all other regions of the UK on drug related issues.  During that meeting there was discussion on the reclassification of ketamine.   Addressing the harms, particularly those which are irreversible, associated with ketamine are priorities across the UK.The classification of drugs is a reserved matter, and any decision will ultimately rest with the UK Government.  I am aware that on 8 January 2025, the Home Office announced that they are seeking expert advice on reclassifying ketamine as a Class A substance.  The Home Office will consult and engage with the devolved administrations on all aspects of drug policy and any future changes.It is important to note that my role as Minister of Justice is about the criminal justice response and enforcement of the law. Representations on the classification or re-classification of drugs would be a matter for the Department of Health, based on the wider health implications.

answer_text: Offences in relation to ‘Possession of a class B drug’ may be dealt with by way of court prosecution or by out-of-court diversionary disposal. question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her Department’s plans to introduce longer sentences for those convicted of assaulting an emergency service worker.
answer_text: I intend to introduce an offence of assaulting a person engaged in performing a public duty, providing a public service or providing services to the public within my Sentencing Bill. This offence will apply to those convicted of assaulting an emergency service worker.The offence will come with a higher maximum penalty in the magistrates’ courts than that currently available for existing similar offences, and, for more serious assaults, the fact that the victim is such a person will become a statutory aggravating factor.Executive agreement to commence drafting the Sentencing Bill was granted on 19th December and I aim to introduce the Bill in the Assembly in the autumn of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail her Department’s spending on translation services in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The amount spent by my Department on translation services has not been recorded by my Department in a way that makes it possible to identify the relevant costs in the specific format requested.The table below outlines expenditure on translation costs for the last five financial years from 2019-20 to 2023-24. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action her Department is taking to promote North Down as a potential filming location for broadcasters and television production companies.
answer_text: NI Screen actively promotes North Down as part of its work to support the local screen industry. Locations such as Clandeboye Estate, Bangor, Holywood, Donaghadee,  Helen’s Bay beach, and the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum feature regularly in screen productions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of new Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools in North Down to be set up for the 2025-26 academic year.
answer_text: The number of specialist provision classes to be established in North Down for the 2025/26 year has yet to be confirmed. My Department and the Education Authority are engaging with schools in North Down, and across Northern Ireland, who have expressed an interest in setting up a class for September 2025 to complete site assessments.  These assessments will confirm the feasibility to deliver the refurbishment works or additional accommodation required to set up a class for September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her engagement with NIE Networks in relation to goodwill payments for customers affected by prolonged periods without electricity.
answer_text: Officials from my Department continue to work with the Utility Regulator and NIE Networks to determine what options are available to provide payments to electricity customers across the North who were most impacted by a loss of power due to Storm Éowyn. A working group, consisting of the Department, Utility Regulator and NIE Networks, is analysing the options in terms of the regulatory arrangements and financial impact.I will keep Assembly colleagues informed on the progress of this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20166/22-27, to detail why she did not provide figures for local council areas when her Department maintains information at that level.
answer_text: Pursuant to AQW 20166/22-27, I can advise that my Department maintains records of salt bins and their locations on a local council area basis for the purposes of their maintenance and replenishment.  My Department does not hold records of requests for salt bins and their fulfilment by local council area in a readily accessible format and to try to provide this information would require an extensive exercise to be conducted across the Department which might only be expected to produce data with a limited degree of exactitude, and this exercise itself would very likely involve disproportionate costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his Department’s ten-year capital plan.
answer_text: The Department’s capital programme aims to modernise and maintain our Health Service, ensuring that the estate is fit for purpose to enable service delivery.The draft Budget for 2025-26 is currently out for consultation and can be accessed at Draft Budget 2025-26 Consultation | Department of Finance.The Department’s proposed capital allocation for 2025/26 is £391 million.  This is less than what is required to fund our committed projects, critical ICT and to maintain our existing services, and does not provide the funding required to commence work on new projects that are deemed essential to protect health services.Without additional investment, it is inevitable that the HSC estate will continue to deteriorate, while the pace at which we can deliver essential new facilities will have to be slowed down to fit within the budget allocation.However, the Department’s current capital budget continues to support a significant capital investment programme. This includes a high level of contractually committed expenditure in 2025/26, and future years, consisting of our flagship projects and existing projects in design or under construction. In addition, there is regular ongoing investment in estate maintenance, and for vehicle fleet for the HSC and Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service (NIFRS), ICT investment, and the replacement of medical equipment.In light of the funding constraints my Department continues to face, the capital investment plan is being reviewed to provide an assessment of capital investment that is likely to be possible in the absence of any significant increase in the level of funding available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what action his Department is taking to address the increase in spending on agency staff within the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: The use of agency workers is a cost effective and necessary way to manage some time bound and project aspects of temporary work and to provide maximum flexibility to support the business needs of the Civil Service and its departments. A combination of pauses to recruitment and a number of reprioritisation exercises given the budgetary situation, staffing the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) contract as well as pressures around implementing the Windsor Framework, have increased the need for temporary agency workers.Individual Departments are responsible for ensuring that the costs associated with agency workers are affordable and to make savings were possible. It is for each Minister to record and report the associated costs in their respective departments. I can however confirm that in my own Department of Finance, in line with current NICS wide guidance, business area before they consider filling the post using an Agency Worker, it must consider all other options of filling the vacancy or new job role. Over the last 2 years, due to the challenging financial environment, general service recruitment was paused leading to a lack of supply in several general service grades.  The resourcing team within my department have plans in place to deliver a full programme of general service recruitment from AO to G6 in a bid to create a supply of permanent staff to fill vacancies throughout all NICS departments.My department encourages its business areas to regularly review its agency worker assignments, and where it is appropriate to end them.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of individuals charged with possession of Ketamine in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Police Service of Northern Ireland publish statistics on numbers of persons charged or summonsed by them as part of their recorded crime statistics. They also issue statistics on drug seizures and arrests for drug related offences.  Issues around the number of individuals charged with possession of ketamine by police is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational responsibility of the Chief Constable and the role of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.You may, therefore, wish to direct any query relating to statistics on the numbers of persons charged by police, as raised in the question, to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current number of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder consultants, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: There is currently no commissioned service for Adult ADHD. Northern, South Eastern, Southern and Western Health and Social Care Trusts currently have no specialist adult ADHD consultants.  Belfast Health and Social Care Trust reconfigured a small resource from within adult teams to process Adult ADHD referrals this is a 0.5 whole time equivalent consultant physiatrist. Regarding provision of service for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), demand for assessment and diagnosis has grown across Trusts.All Trusts are committed to work closely and collaboratively with commissioners to develop innovative, cost effective, co-produced, quality, and evidenced based services that can meet the needs of adults with ADHD within Trust areas. This work would include the development of clinical pathways.The Department is commissioning a research exercise into the current and projected demand with a view to bringing forward recommendations to inform a commissioned ADHD service, subject to future budget availability. The exercise will (amongst other things) seek to establish the prevalence of ADHD (for both children and adults) in Northern Ireland, examine best practice from other jurisdictions and identify challenges and barriers in relation to ADHD diagnosis and treatment. Importantly, it will be expected to make recommendations on what any future commissioned service might look like in terms of, for example, costs, staffing profiles and recommended care pathways.A research lead has now been identified and work on the research has commenced, with an anticipated completion date of end June 2025.Any future commissioned service will be subject to both budget availability and evidenced demand, but the research, once completed, will help provide a firm evidence base to inform a future model.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of deaths recorded where Ketamine use was cited as the main cause of death in each of the last five years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: NISRA collates data on death registrations as collected by the General Register Office.  Drug-related deaths are coded and reported according to the World Health Organisation (WHO) International Classification of Diseases (ICD tenth revision) using codes F11–F16, F18–F19, X40–X44, X60–X64, X85 and Y10–Y14. It is not possible, from the information provided on a death certificate, to determine which drug was the main cause of death. Information is however available on the number of deaths where Ketamine was mentioned on the death certificate and therefore contributed to death.Between 1 January 2020 and 30 June 2024 (latest information available) Ketamine was mentioned on the death certificate of 12 registered deaths, two in 2020, one each in 2021 and 2022, five in 2023 and three in the first six months of 2024. Due to the small numbers and associated risk of disclosure a breakdown of the requested information by individual parliamentary constituency cannot be provided. The information has been provided for the total five years by grouped parliamentary constituency.Parliamentary Constituency (grouped)Number of Ketamine-related drug deathsBelfast North, East, South and West3North, East and South Antrim3East Londonderry, Mid Ulster, Foyle1Fermanagh and South Tyrone, West Tyrone, Newry and Armagh and Upper Bann2Lagan Valley, South Down, Strangford and North Down3Total12Statistics on drug-related and drug misuse deaths 2013-2023 tables are scheduled to be published by early March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail any outstanding wait time data within the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust as a result of the roll-out of encompass; and (ii) to provide a timescale for when said data will be available.
answer_text: I am advised by the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust that there are currently no outstanding waiting list submissions / wait-time data for South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust, due to the implementation of encompass.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action her Department is taking to improve the uptake of apprenticeships in Ards and North Down Borough Council area.
answer_text: My department promotes apprenticeship programmes across the North, promotion of apprenticeships does not differ from one council area to another.My department runs a suite of advertising campaigns biannually which raise awareness of apprenticeships for both prospective apprentices and employers, and encourage uptake of these programmes.My department also facilitates the annual NI Apprenticeship Week which, in 2025 consisted of over 60 public events being held across the North from 3-7 February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department has had with Queen's University Belfast in relation to the rationale behind the decision to open a campus in India.
answer_text: The decision was taken by Queen’s University Belfast, which is an autonomous institution responsible for its own business strategy. No funding has been provided by the Department in relation to this new campus.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any strategic interventions contained in his Department’s Road Safety Action Plan 2024-25 that (i) are successfully completed (ii) progress has been made and being carried forward; and (iii) are incomplete.
answer_text: All Strategic Interventions in the 2024-25 action plan are progressing.  Following the end of the 2024-25 reporting period, an assessment will be made on all the Interventions to determine their status, progress and in what form they will progress into the following year’s action plan. A report summarising the progress made throughout 2024-25 will be available Summer 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the average hotel occupancy rate during 2024, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: NISRA has published provisional hotel occupancy rates for January – December 2024 at this link. Table 5 shows these rates by local council area.NISRA will publish final hotel occupancy rates in May 2025 allowing for any late returns and revisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline what action his Department is taking to educate young people on the potential harms associated with gambling.
To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any engagement she has had with Link and Cash Action Group in relation to the provision of banking hubs in North Down.
answer_text: In her engagements with the banking sector, my predecessor Dr Caoimhe Archibald MLA, met with both LINK and Cash Access UK, a body set up following a recommendation from the Cash Action Group to provide cash and basic banking services in communities. In those meetings, she pressed on all of the banking representatives to play their role in maintaining access to cash and vital banking services, including the timely roll out of banking hubs in areas impacted by bank closures.I understand that the North now has five established Banking hubs, including a hub in Comber in the Ards and North Down Borough Council Area. I plan to visit the Warrenpoint Banking Hub next month, where I will meet with Cash Access UK and press on the need for appropriate in-person access to banking services for those areas losing bank branches and cash access facilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the total value of rates relief provided to businesses in each of the last five years, broken down by local council area.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action his Department is taking to encourage overnight visitors from Great Britain as part of the Tourism Action and Vision Plan: 10 Year Plan.
answer_text: Tourism Ireland is committed to increasing the value of overseas tourism by inspiring visitors via advertising, publicity and broadcast shows, with specific segments targeted in Britain. Tourism Ireland has strategic partnerships with air and sea carriers, promoting major sporting events such as The Open which returns to Portrush this summer, and develop links between local tourism and tour operators and business in Britain, who can bring visitors at scale.Tourism Ireland in Britain links local tourism businesses with tour operators and travel trade, who can bring visitors at scale. For example, 17 local tourism businesses took part in Tourism Ireland’s Celtic Connections workshop in Glasgow in January and around 20 local tourism businesses join Tourism Ireland at World Travel Market in London each year. As part of a broader Business Events strategy 17 local tourism businesses took part in Tourism Ireland’s ‘Ireland meets the West End’ B2B event in London last year, an event which aims to grow business events specifically from Britain.Tourism Ireland co-produces travel shows designed to inspire travel here, including on both BBC and ITV this year.		Tourism Ireland’s work is critical to the growth of our tourism industry. It is vital that it receives sufficient resources to continue to deliver effective marketing within core key markets and key segments such as Britain, hence my predecessors decision to provide additional funding and restore the funding ratio to Tourism Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on any plans to address the backlog in the Planning Appeals Commission.
answer_text: As advised in AQW 11976/22-27 and AQW 12932/22-27 the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC) is an independent tribunal body.  Its operation is the responsibility of the Chief Commissioner. It would not be appropriate for me as Minister of Justice to comment on matters that encroach on that independence.PAC’s performance is included in its Annual Review prepared by the Chief Commissioner. The 1 April 2023 – 31 March 2024 Annual Review reported that the Commission has increased the number of decisions delivered year on year while the number of appeals on hand has decreased.  The Chief Commissioner also gave a commitment to increase the quantum of cases disposed of within the 2024/25 financial year.  The PAC annual review is available at:   The Planning and Water Appeals Commissions. Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) is responsible for providing resources and services to PAC and is accountable for the use of these resources and associated expenditure, whilst at the same time respecting the operational independence of PAC.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the appointment of independent commissioners to alleviate pressures on the Planning Appeals Commission.
answer_text: I am awaiting the outcome of my Department’s bid to the Interim Public Sector Transformation Board seeking funding for independent inspectors to alleviate pressures on the Planning Appeals Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) his Department's plans to restore the damaged woodland within Cairn Wood; and (ii) any work with Ards and North Down Borough Council to progress reopening the facility following Storm Éowyn.
answer_text: Forest Service is undertaking a full analysis of the extent of woodlands damaged across the forest estate by Storm Éowyn. The initial assessment indicates that Cairn Wood is one of many forests significantly impacted by the storm, which resulted in public access being restricted for public safety reasons.Ards and North Down Borough Council operate the car park provision and recreational trails at Cairn Wood under licence and lease arrangements with my Department.  Forest Service and Council officials have initially discussed the impacts, however the extent of works needed to restore public access, and any necessary tree replacement has still to be quantified.  Forest Service will continue to work collaboratively with the Council to secure the re-opening of facilities as soon as possible when the assessments have been completed.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the Right Care, Right Person policy in conjunction with the PSNI and the Department of Health.
answer_text: My Departmental officials sit on a Strategic Oversight Group with the Department of Health and other stakeholders to identify any policy or other issues for the Department arising from the development of the PSNI’s RCRP policy. RCRP itself is an operational matter for the PSNI.  Work on developing the policy to deliver RCRP is being taken forward by the Operational Silver Group, of which the DoJ is not a member, reflecting the PSNI’s operational independence from DoJ. You may, therefore, wish to consider approaching the PSNI for a more detailed update on RCRP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any responsibility his Department has in relation to the regulation of the availability of helium.
answer_text: In the United Kingdom, the regulation of helium for medical use falls under the Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). Helium used for medical purposes, is classified as a medicinal product and regulated under the Human Medicines Regulations 2012. The MHRA ensures that medical-grade helium meets strict purity and safety standards before being used in hospitals. The use of medicinal helium falls under the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health (COSHH) Regulations 2002, ensuring safe handling in healthcare settings.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the funding made available to councils in aid of Local Economic Partnerships, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: Funding allocation for the Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs) was formally announced at Newry Mourne and Down’s inaugural LEP meeting on 27th January. Details of fund allocation and rationale can be found on the following webpage  Regional balance fund | Department for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 10865/22-27, to detail the reasons for the delay in the opening of the Urgent Care Centre at the Ulster Hospital until June 2025.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his rationale for the level of funding allocated to Ards and North Down Borough Council in aid of Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: I formally announced a breakdown of funding allocations on 27th January. Details of fund allocations and rationale can be found on the following webpage  Regional balance fund | Department for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on their engagement with NIE Networks in relation to the provision of goodwill payments for those who have been without electricity for prolonged periods.
answer_text: We are mindful of the ongoing significant impact on the thousands of people without power as a result of Storm Éowyn.  We met with the Managing Director from Northern Ireland Electricity Networks (NIE) on Saturday 25 January, and at the Executive meeting on Monday 27 January urged him to consider how NIE could provide support to those families and businesses impacted by the effects of Storm Éowyn. Since the Executive meeting we have received an update from NIE that they have had exploratory conversations with the Department for Economy and the Utility Regulator.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of part-time on call firefighter vacancies in Holywood Fire Station.
answer_text: There are 10 part-time on call firefighter vacancies at Holywood Fire Station. NIFRS is currently recruiting for this location, with an open evening in Holywood Fire Station, 4 February 2025 at 8pm.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with mobile network operators in relation to improving connectivity during severe weather conditions.
answer_text: Telecommunications policy is a reserved matter, with the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) holding policy responsibility on improvements to mobile connectivity.      My officials maintain regular contact with DSIT and representatives of the mobile communications industry on telecommunications matters ensuring that all issues are given appropriate local consideration regarding mobile connectivity improvement here.Mobile infrastructure is currently provided on a wholly commercial basis by Mobile Network Operators (MNOs), however, my officials are involved in several initiatives which will help improve mobile connectivity here, including the Northern Ireland Barrier Busting Taskforce and the Shared Rural Network. In relation to the disruption caused by severe weather conditions, DfE officials met with The Executive Office’s Recovery Coordination Group, which includes representatives from mobile network operators, to discuss action by Northern Ireland Electricity (NIE) and the MNOs to restore mobile services.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the amount of funding that will be made available to Ards and North Down Borough Council through the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Local Change Fund.
answer_text: Ards and North Down Borough Council will receive total funding of £180,000. This includes allocations under both the Momentum Fund 2024/25 (£60,000) and the Local Change Fund for 2025/26 (£120,000).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the amount of funding provided by her Department to Thompson House, Belfast, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Thompson House Belfast is one of seven Approved Premises which act as a transition between prison and resettlement back into the community for high-risk offenders leaving custody. My Department has provided the following funding to Thompson House in each of the last five financial years:

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline their Department’s engagement plans with the new Government administration in the United States of America.
answer_text: Northern Ireland has enjoyed a special relationship with successive US Administrations through our shared history, through the peace process and through our ever increasing economic proposition.As well as benefitting from US investment, we can increasingly contribute to US growth in areas such as technology, creative industries and life sciences. Through the Northern Ireland Bureau we are developing relationships with key stakeholders to highlight the business, tourism, cultural, sporting and university opportunities that Northern Ireland offers the US.We will travel to North Carolina on a trade mission visit. The deputy First Minister will travel to Washington for St Patrick’s Day events.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any engagement he has had with the Minister of Education in relation to identifying schools for inclusion in a further tranche of the part-time 20mph speed limit at schools scheme.
answer_text: There has been no engagement with the Minister of Education in relation to identifying schools for inclusion in a further tranche of the part-time 20mph speed limit at schools’ scheme. As you are aware, the recently published review report recommends that the Department should provide a third tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools, however this will be more modest in size. The report also concludes that a more targeted approach is needed to ensure that the correct safety intervention is selected, and work updating our policy document is underway. All remaining schools will be assessed in line with the revised policy and any subsequent engagement taken forward directly between my Department and the schools identified for part time speed limits.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any assessment his Department has carried out on the impact that the reopening of the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme would have on waiting lists.
answer_text: The Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (RoIRS) was introduced on 30 June 2021 as part of the Elective Care Framework to help reduce waiting lists in Northern Ireland (NI). The scheme was a limited 12-month administrative version of the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive which ceased to apply to NI following the UK’s exit from the EU. A bid for funding for a replacement scheme was submitted during the 2024/25 budget setting process as part of a wider bid for £135million of additional funding to tackle waiting lists. My Department assessed that a new reimbursement scheme could have treated over 4,000 patients this financial year. Unfortunately, under the 2024/25 Budget, no additional funding was provided for tackling our waiting lists and the focus has had to be on supporting those on cancer and time critical waiting lists. In addition, the draft budget for 2025/26 does not provide any additional funding for waiting list reduction initiatives. As such, there is no funding available at present to reinstate a cross-border reimbursement scheme. Any future funding allocation to a similar scheme to help reduce waiting lists would need to be considered as part of a broader review of resource investment in Waiting List Initiatives, prioritised based on how to maximise clinical outcome and value for money.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is considering reopening the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme.
answer_text: The Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (RoIRS) was introduced on 30 June 2021 as part of the Elective Care Framework to help reduce waiting lists in Northern Ireland (NI). The scheme was a limited 12-month administrative version of the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive which ceased to apply to NI following the UK’s exit from the EU. A bid for funding for a replacement scheme was submitted during the 2024/25 budget setting process as part of a wider bid for £135million of additional funding to tackle waiting lists. It was intended that a new reimbursement scheme could have treated over 4,000 patients this financial year. 	Unfortunately, under the 2024/25 Budget, no additional funding was provided for tackling our waiting lists and the focus has had to be on supporting those on cancer and time critical waiting lists. In addition, the draft budget for 2025/26 does not provide any additional funding for waiting list reduction initiatives. 		As such, there is no funding available at present to reinstate a cross-border reimbursement scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action his Department is taking to support the expansion of Belfast Harbour to allow it to act as a turnaround port.
answer_text: My Department and I fully endorse and support the expansion of Belfast Harbour to allow it to act as a turnaround port.I was delighted to accept the invitation to speak at the launch of the Belfast Harbour Strategy on 21 January 2025, at which these ambitious plans were announced. We see Belfast Harbour, and its future ambitions, as a key player in the delivery of my economic vision of an innovative, more sustainable, regionally balanced economy which provides good jobs.Prior to the launch of the Belfast Harbour Strategy, I met with Dr Theresa Donaldson, Chair, Belfast Harbour with DfI Minister O’Dowd on 29 August 2024 to discuss Belfast Harbour’s intention to request a joint bid, with the Port of Cork Company, to Shared Island funding in respect of this catalytic project.The Shared Island funding bid is to support a record single investment, of £90m, by Belfast Harbour, to develop a deep-water quay, a new terminal to boost cruise tourism business and new offshore renewable energy facilities. The deep-water quay and new terminal would allow cruise turnaround business and overnight stays, increase visitor overnights pre and post cruise, and permit the largest cruise ships to dock at the quay. Belfast Harbour has also played a leading role in recent years in supporting new, innovative sectors of economy, including investment in production facilities at the Belfast Harbour Studios, creating a step change in the capacity of our film and TV industry. This will be further enhanced through the new £72m “state of the art” virtual production facilities at Studio Ulster, which have been supported through the Belfast City Region Growth Deal.My Department and I very much welcome the continuation of Belfast Harbour’s commitment to invest in facilities which enhance the regional economy and the recently announced plans to support the growth of the cruise tourism sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail whether the Northern Ireland Prison Service carries out a formal risk assessment process when considering transgender prison placements.
answer_text: A risk assessment is completed for everyone committed into prison, irrespective of how they identify. As with all prisoners, when a person who is transgender is committed to custody by the Courts, the Prison Service will consider their specific needs on a case-by-case basis following committal to an establishment as directed by the court.In regards to a transgender person, a case conference will be convened, chaired by the Director of Prisons and with representatives from the Senior Management teams of the male and female prison estates. This takes a risk-based approach on decisions around accommodation and care that is person-centred, and informed by advice from colleagues in the South Eastern Health & Social Care Trust (SET) who are responsible for the delivery of healthcare services to people in prison. The Case Conference is the formal record for each individual case.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how the additional £13.6 million of capital funding, acquired through the January Monitoring Round, will be allocated within his Department.
answer_text: The additional £13.6m of capital funding allocated to my Department through the January Monitoring Round has allowed me to continue with planned schemes this financial year, with £12m being used to address the remaining opening overplanning commitment. The balance of funding has been allocated to Translink towards the Belfast Transport Hub.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of transgender prisoner placements the Northern Ireland Prison Service has considered in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The number of occasions when the Northern Ireland Prison Service has considered the accommodation of transgender prisoners in each of the last five years is so low that disclosure could lead to the identification of individuals.  On that basis, disclosure of this information would contravene the individuals’ rights under data protection principles.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the dental practices in North Down accepting NHS patients.
answer_text: The BSO have records of which General Dental Practitioners (GDPs) have a contract with the Department of Health to provide NHS treatment, and in which practices they work. The BSO does not have information as to the capacity of each GDP’s register.Patients can find a list of all General Dental Practices with at least one practitioner contracted to provide NHS treatment at http://online.hscni.net/other-hsc-organisations/hsc-service-finder. It can be used to search for dental practices in the patient’s area, sorted by those closest to their address. A contact number for each dental practice is available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the destinations under consideration for the development of a Stewardship Plan in 2025 as part of his Department’s Tourism Vision and Action Plan.
answer_text: Regional Balance is one of my key priorities in the Tourism Vision & Action Plan: 10 Year Plan, launched on 15 January 2025.  Tourism NI is in the early stages of developing a Destination Stewardship Framework to sustainably develop tourism destinations across the North. Tourism NI is currently procuring services to support the co-creation of the Destination Stewardship Framework. This process will include consultation with key stakeholders, including Ards & North Down Borough Council and tourism providers in the Strangford Lough area.A pilot Destination Stewardship Plan will be proposed, based on one of the tourism destinations identified as part of the Framework development. This pilot destination plan will be selected by the specialist contractor in partnership with Tourism NI, using appropriate research, data and evidence. The pilot plan will inform all further destination plans across the North in the coming years.
