question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the impact that the provision of dedicated funding for General Practitioners' indemnity costs will have on GP services.
answer_text: I was pleased to recently give my approval to the 2024/25 General Medical Services (GMS) contract. This new contract is an important step forward to achieving greater stability in General Practice. It makes significant progress including provision of dedicated funding for indemnity costs. A key aim of the new contract arrangements is to provide GPs with greater certainty over their income throughout the year. The specific provision of £5million of funding towards indemnity costs is part of this overall basket of measures designed to support the ongoing sustainability of primary care. In the context of a very disappointing budget settlement for my Department, I believe that this ring-fenced allocation will make an important contribution towards supporting GPs with their clinical negligence indemnity costs. However, it is important to be aware that the issues impacting the sustainability of General Practice are much broader than indemnity provision. We need to acknowledge that there are a range of issues facing general practice which are complex and will require a sustained, long-term response. The provision of support for indemnity costs as part of the 24/25 General Medical Services contract is just one of a range of measures that are being implemented to secure the ongoing sustainability of general practice locally. Despite the Budgetary position, my Department is committed to exploring how we can make primary care and General Practice more resilient; improve demand management and access to GP-led services; and strengthen the GP workforce. As an Assembly, we need to work together to ensure that we get the funding we need for health and social care so that we can ensure the long-term sustainability of provision for those that we serve.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s spend on the Royal Belfast Hospital for Sick Children’s cystic fibrosis unit in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is an inherited disease affecting, and in due course, damaging the lungs and digestive tract.  Children with CF are supported by a dedicated multidisciplinary team in RBHSC with medical, nursing, AHP, pharmacy and social care input.  There are a range of treatments which help control symptoms and prevent or reduce complications.Spend within the unit is profiled across staffing infrastructure and medications including those which target gene mutations.As advised in the response to your previous request (AQW 10700/22-27) these medications are purchased through a Commercial Access Agreement, with the price of the medications being covered by a Patient Access Scheme.  These are confidential pricing agreements agreed between pharmaceutical companies and the Department of Health in England (with input from NICE). As such it is not therefore possible to release the spend on the CF unit without breaching this confidentiality agreement on CF drugs.Staffing infrastructure is outwith this position and can be released.  Spend on staffing will vary from year to year due to turnover and operational issues however the Department can confirm that in 19/20, the funded recurrent baseline for staffing in the paediatric unit was £0.77m.  This was increased in 2020 by an additional recurrent investment of £0.51m to secure additional staffing to support the introduction of new specialist medicines, taking the recurrent baseline infrastructure funding to £1.28m.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s spend on the adult cystic fibrosis unit in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is an inherited disease affecting, and in due course, damaging the lungs and digestive tract.  Adults with CF are supported by a dedicated multidisciplinary team with medical, nursing, AHP, pharmacy and social care input.  There are a range of treatments which help control symptoms and prevent or reduce complications.Spend within the unit is profiled across staffing infrastructure and medications including those which target gene mutations.As advised in the response to your previous request (AQW 10700/22-27) these medications are purchased through a Commercial Access Agreement, with the price of the medications being covered by a Patient Access Scheme.  These are confidential pricing agreements agreed between pharmaceutical companies and the Department of Health in England (with input from NICE). As such it is not therefore possible to release the spend on the CF unit without breaching this confidentiality agreement on CF drugs.Staffing infrastructure is outwith this position and can be released.  Spend on staffing will vary from year to year due to turnover and operational issues however the Department can confirm that in 19/20, the funded recurrent baseline for staffing in the adult unit was £1.03m.  This was increased in 2020 by an additional recurrent investment of £0.52m to secure additional staffing to support the introduction of new specialist medicines, taking the recurrent baseline infrastructure funding to £1.55m.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement he has had with VisitBritain in relation to his plans to grow Northern Ireland’s tourism industry.
answer_text: Tourism NI has a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) in place with VisitBritain covering both consumer and business tourism promotional activities and collaborative corporate workstreams. Last year the North was featured in 51 pieces of promotional media coverage generated by VisitBritain, as well as hosting specific web pages promoting the North as a tourism destination as part of the relaunched VisitBritain website.  Tourism NI works with VisitBritain to promote local tourism experience providers through the VisitBritain Shop,  an online sales platform which features visitor attractions and experiences. Some local visitor attractions and experiences also partnered with VisitBritain in 2023 on a number of  marketing and sales campaigns. Tourism NI also cooperate with VisitBritain through its participation on some  regional operational boards and in knowledge sharing across a number of business areas including marketing, business development and data analyticsTourism NI, alongside Visit Belfast and Visit Derry, participated in the VisitBritain Business Events Association Conference in February 2024. This was an opportunity to promote and sell the North as a leading international conference destination.Both Tourism NI and Tourism Ireland are currently working in collaboration with VisitBritain to produce an Accessibility and Inclusivity Toolkit which will support a consistent approach to presenting accessibility information to consumers across Britain and Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the impact the opening of the Urgent Care Centre in the Ulster Hospital will have on pressures on the hospital’s Emergency Department.
answer_text: The benefits of a co-located Urgent Care Centre with the South Eastern Trust’s Emergency Department are clearly laid out in the Trust’s consultation document that is still available online. Public-Consultation-on-line-version-08-02-2023.pdf (hscni.net)The Urgent Care Centre will be staffed by a team of highly skilled clinicians including Emergency Nurse Practitioners, Advanced Nurse Practitioners, Allied Health Professionals and senior medical staff, working together on one site.  The role of the Emergency Nurse Practitioner (ENP) will be further developed with the support of other medical staff allowing them to see more complex cases as well as minor injuries.   This concentration of expertise will also have ready access to a wide, comprehensive range of diagnostics on site.  Furthermore, there will be no age restriction on who can be treated at the urgent care centre that will provide for adults and children.On site triaging will ensure that patients who require urgent care or minor injury treatment access appropriate care in a more timely manner.Having transferred minor injury services to the Ulster Hospital and developed an Enhanced Minor Injury service on that site, construction has commenced on the urgent care centre. Once opened, like the current Enhanced Minor injury Unit, it will treat patients 8am to 6pm, 7 days a week including bank holidays.  All patients will be seen at the Emergency Department outside of these hours which will remain open 24 hours a day 7 days a week.Overall, the Urgent Care Centre will provide better, more timely and appropriate care in a purpose-built facility that is sustainable and safer for the local community.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of complaints the South Eastern Health and Social Trust has received in relation to operations carried out by private service providers.
answer_text: Further to previous questions on this issue (AQW 9722 and AQW 10775) the South Eastern HSC Trust uses private providers to deliver endoscopy and cystoscopes at weekends as well as ophthalmology and dermatology treatments.  These patients are on HSC waiting lists.No complaints have been received in 2024/25 to the end of April.  In 2023/24, however, the Trust received 3 formal complaints about services provided by Independent Sector Providers.All providers contracted by the Trust must deal with complaints they receive directly with the patient in line with HSC Regional Complaints Policy and Regulatory requirements.  The Trust is only involved where the complaint is not resolved satisfactorily.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of schools in North Down that have expressed an interest in facilitating specialist provision classes.
answer_text: My department recently issued a short survey to all schools inviting them to express their interest in establishing specialist provision classes in mainstream schools (SPiMS) for September 2024 and beyond.Sixteen schools in the North Down area responded to the survey, five of which expressed an interest in establishing new or additional specialist provision classes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his assessment of the impact of the Footways (Prohibition of Waiting) Order (Northern Ireland) 2023; and (ii) whether he has any plans to increase restrictions on parking on pavements.
answer_text: The introduction of the Footways (Prohibition of Waiting) Order (Northern Ireland) 2023 allows the Department to enforce against vehicles found parked on footways alongside bus lanes, bus stops/stands, mandatory cycle lanes and mandatory ‘School Keep Clear’ markings.  The legislation is intended to bring clarity and I believe it is a positive step forward in deterring inconsiderate pavement parking. The Department routinely reminds drivers to ‘think before you park’ and continues to take action against vehicles that are in contravention of parking restrictions, including those that apply to footways.On 6 December 2023 the Department released a Findings Report on the responses received to the consultation on inconsiderate pavement parking. This can be accessed using this link;https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/inconsiderate-pavement-parking-findings-report  I plan to issue a formal response to the consultation exercise when I have had time to consider the Findings Report.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will review the minimum intervention level for potholes following the publication of the Budget 2024-25.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.  In response, my Department has had to reduce its routine road maintenance activities to a limited service, which prioritises delivery of essential services for maintaining public safety and protecting the transport network. Consequently, only the highest priority defects across the network are currently being repaired.The level of repairs that can be carried out in any financial year depends on the funding available to my Department. Until such times as sufficient funding is made available, it will not be possible to change the current level of service. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any delays to the opening of the Hydebank MOT Test Centre; and (ii) the cause of any delays.
answer_text: The construction of the new test centre at Hydebank is now completed.  The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) is going through a rigorous process of testing the newly installed equipment and software.Whilst Hydebank has already opened for driver testing and it is anticipated to open for vehicle testing later this year, this cannot happen until my officials and I are satisfied that the new equipment is safe to use and meets the requirements set out in the contract specification.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he intends to increase his Department’s spending on road safety advertising following his Department’s budget allocation for 2024-25.
answer_text: Budget allocations have not yet been finalised.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the provision of outstanding waiting time data not currently available due to the rollout of the Encompass System in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Department of Health and the South Eastern Trust are in the final stages of validation checks and it is therefore planned that Waiting Times for the Trust will be included as provisional statistics within the next publication.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the creation of a new Urgent Care Centre in the Ulster Hospital; and (ii) whether it will be operational in 2025.
answer_text: The South Eastern Trust has confirmed that progress is being made on the development of an Urgent Care Centre (UCC) at the Ulster Hospital, Dundonald.				(i)	The design of the new facilities adjacent to the Emergency Department has been completed along with the tendering process and the contractor has commenced work on site.				(ii)	It is expected that the new Urgent Care Centre will have opened as planned by late January 2025

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the introduction of baby loss certificates.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department’s plans to support community transport in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: I recognise the importance of community transport to those who have difficulties accessing scheduled public transport options and the value they attach to it.  The grant supported Dial-a-Lift (DAL) transport service in rural areas and Disability Action Transport Scheme (DATS) in urban areas both help improve access to health appointments, shops and facilitates, voluntary groups, friends and family and enables people to be independent and remain connected to their local communities.While funding for community transport is not part of my Department’s statutory defined spend, in the absence of an agreed budget at the beginning of the financial year, I approved interim funding for both DAL and DATS for April to June 2024 based on 2023/24 funding levels to support continued provision of community transport. 2024/25 allocations are currently being considered.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the Rural Micro Capital Grant Scheme.
answer_text: My officials have completed an evaluation of the 2022 Rural Micro Capital Grant Scheme. The findings, lessons learned and recommendations from the 2022 evaluation and the independent evaluation of the schemes delivered between 2016 and 2021 have been considered and a Business Case proposing to launch the Scheme in 2024/2025 is at an advanced stage.Subject to my consideration and in conjunction with wider budget discussions I hope to be in a position to make an announcement regarding the future of the Rural Micro Capital Grant Scheme in the coming weeks.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the monitoring and oversight that is carried out by his Department on private service providers performing weekend operations in the Ulster Hospital.
answer_text: As previously described in my response to an earlier question on this matter (AQW 9722), The South Eastern HSC Trust uses private providers to deliver endoscopy and cystoscopes at weekends as well as ophthalmology and dermatology treatments.  These patients are on HSC waiting lists.The South Eastern HSC Trust uses a regionally developed contract for all services provided on Trust premises by private health care providers, as part of waiting list initiative work.  The Contract outlines the obligations for both the provider and the Trust and the associated performance management and oversight required.  The Trust oversees service delivery in line with the contract and employs a Governance Nurse for each area involved.  This ensures that the service is provided in line with expectations.  This person also holds the keys to the Trust premises.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department’s spending on the Rural Transport Fund in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Please see details of grant allocated under the Rural Transport Fund for each of the last five years.2019/202020/212021/22CDM Community Transport£417,983£422,611£406,900Down District Accessible Transport£301,787£297,120£297,120Easilink Community Transport£381,690£306,723£306,723Fermanagh Community Transport£326,180£321,980£321,980Lagan Valley Rural Transport£119,064£115,330£115,330North Coast Community Transport£477,194£384,894£384,894South Antrim Community Transport£120,205£117,871£117,871Southern Area Partnership£294,128£285,262£285,262Community Transport Association£55,100£55,100£55,100 2022/232023/24CDM Community Transport£406,900£393,337Down District Accessible Transport£297,120£287,216Easilink Community Transport£306,723£296,499Fermanagh Community Transport£321,980£234,777Lagan Valley Rural Transport£115,330£56,704North Coast Community Transport£384,894£372,064South Antrim Community Transport£117,871£113,942Southern Area Partnership£285,262£275,752Community Transport Association£55,100£55,100

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of patients deregistered from NHS dental practices in North Down in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Table 1 overleaf shows the number of patients deregistered from active NHS dental practices in the North Down Assembly Area in each of the last five years.A patient’s dental registration lasts 25 months from the date they were first accepted by the General Dental Practitioner (GDP) or the date the arrangement was last extended. With capacity within General Dental Services (GDS) significantly reduced throughout the pandemic, to prevent large numbers of patient registrations lapsing as a result of them being unable to obtain a dental appointment, the duration of patient registrations was extended between March 2020 and March 2023.With no patient registrations naturally lapsing, the number of de-registrations via the standard WCA 966 form has increased. GDPs may choose to de-register patients for a number of reasons. For example, in 2022 a practice in Donaghadee was unable to replace a practitioner who left the practice and as a result the patients were de-registered. It is possible that some of these patients subsequentially re-registered with their original dentist at their new practice which was however outside North Down.Table 1: Number of Patient De-registrations from an active health service dentist in the North Down Assembly Area by Year1,2,3,4Year4Number of Patient De-registrations12019620208202180620222,3762023548Source: Family Practitioner Services Dental Payment System, Family Practitioner Services, HSC Business Services Organisation.  1 To de-register a patient, a dentist must submit a WCA 966 form to the BSO and give the patient 3 months’ notice. The count of de-registrations is based on the number of WCA 966 forms submitted and recorded on the FPS Dental Payment System. The variable used to signify the submissions of a WCA 966 form may also be used in other scenarios e.g. removing a registration from a dentist that should have been seen on referral rather than an initial or re-registration. It is not possible to specifically extract WCA 966 forms submitted, thus resulting in a possible overcount of true de-registrations.2 An active dentist has made an arrangement with the SPPG (previously the Health and Social Care Board) to provide health service dentistry.3 Private patients who have been de-registered are not included in this data. 4 All count figures represent de-registrations across each full calendar year (1st January to 31st December). The data presented is as of 24th April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department intends to introduce real-time water quality reports.
answer_text: The Regulatory requirements for Water Quality Status Reports sets out the monitoring in six-year cycles with interim reports mid-way through the cycle, approximately every three years.  The regulations are designed to consider the long-term view of water quality and this frequency is deemed the best time frame to observe improvements or deteriorations in water quality.  In addition to regulatory monitoring, my Department actively commissions the monitoring of selected chemicals and physical properties of waterbodies at higher frequencies using remote sensing technologies.  This higher frequency monitoring in targeted priority catchments provides more detailed data for investigations and studies which do not form part of the water quality status reports. There would be limited benefit from producing real time data for all parameters for water quality status due to the slow change in some parameters and at present there are no plans to do so.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the numbers of patients in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust receiving care for cystic fibrosis in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Information on the numbers of patients in the Belfast Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust receiving care for cystic fibrosis is not available in the Department. The data in table 1 has been provided by the Belfast HSC Trust.Table 1. Number of patients receiving care for cystic fibrosis in the Belfast HSC Trust in each of the last five yearsDate*Number receiving care29/04/202433113/11/202333030/06/202233228/07/202132509/07/2020316Source: Belfast HSC Trust*Patient list is not updated on the same date each year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s spending on cystic fibrosis treatment drugs in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Most patients with Cystic Fibrosis in Northern Ireland are treated with medications which target gene mutations.  These are manufactured by a single pharmaceutical company.  These medications are purchased through a Commercial Access Agreement, with the price of the medications being covered by a Patient Access Scheme.  These are confidential pricing agreements agreed between pharmaceutical companies and the Department of Health in England (with input from NICE).It is not therefore possible to release the spend on Cystic Fibrosis drugs without breaching this confidentiality agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the action his Department is taking to promote golf tourism.
answer_text: The value of golf tourism to the North in 2023 was £68.2 million. This has been achieved by promoting golf tourism at trade shows and directly to the consumer through various marketing channels.Over the past 12 months Tourism NI (TNI), working in partnership with Tourism Ireland (TI), has undertaken consumer marketing including digital, print and in-person promotion in all target markets. TNI also regularly runs consumer campaigns in partnership with major online golf travel agencies such as YourGolfTravel, GolfNow and GolfBreaks to encourage golfers to take short breaks here.   The agencies’ golf teams are regularly present on promotional stands at USPGA and DP World Tour events with a focus on promoting golf in the North directly to golfing audiences in the US, UK, Europe and UAE markets, as well as attending targeted trade shows in the same markets. The agencies are also joined by representatives from local golf clubs, tour operators and accommodation providers.  Golf Influencers with large social followings are also used to promote the destination and golf product directly to golfing audiences in the USA, Britain and Europe. In October 2023, TNI hosted the International Association of Golf Tour Operators “Golf in Ireland” Convention at The Ebrington Hotel in Derry, pairing forty international golf tour operators with forty local golf partners, as part of a  3-day familiarisation trip.Finally, my Department has provided TNI with the funding necessary to secure the return of the Irish Open to Royal County Down this year and the British Open next year, which will further promote the North as a world class golfing destination to large global audiences.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action his Department is taking to promote the North Down coastal path as part of Northern Ireland’s tourist offering.
answer_text: Tourism NI (TNI) has regularly promoted North Down as a region through its seasonal marketing campaigns, targeted at specific market segments based on extensive research. One of the most recent pieces of promotional activity was the launch of a major Spring advertising campaign on 22 January 2024 which ran until 31 March.  The campaign featured TV, radio, press, press supplements, outdoor, social and digital advertising, all underpinned by a comprehensive PR strategy.  Planning is in train for the next campaign due to take place in autumn, and with regional balance one of TNI’s key priorities, North Down will again be featured. TNI also works with its partner Tourism Ireland (TI) to host international media, with North Down featuring regularly in many media itineraries. The North Down Coastal path was featured in the itinerary of a recent media fam trip in February 2024, undertaken by the ‘Curious Pixie’, a GB travel influencer whose website and content are viewed in over 120 countries.  TNI has an ongoing programme of PR activity, with tourism businesses and visitor experiences from North Down regularly featuring in PR content, media trips, and commissioned TV programmes on Virgin Media TV. One recent example an Irish Times article commissioned by TNI, called ‘Leap into a romantic getaway in Northern Ireland’. The North Down Coastal Path was specifically mentioned as a ‘must see’ place to visit. TNI also works closely with the tourism industry in North Down and across the North to support the development of engaging and immersive tourism experiences.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on financial contributions from institutions to the Historical Institutional Abuse Redress Board's compensation scheme.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether Northern Ireland has received a Barnett consequential from the flood and coastal erosion risk management investment plan.
answer_text: Funding for this programme was provided to DEFRA as part of the 2020 and 2021 Spending Review settlements, for which the Executive will have received Barnett consequentials at the time.However, as Spending Review Barnett consequentials are assessed at English departmental level and not at programme level, it is therefore not possible to provide specific details of the Barnett consequentials the Executive received for this programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action his Department is taking to improve public access to GP appointments.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the York Street Interchange Project.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times, increase reliability and improve road safety.In October 2022 I published a Placemaking and Active Travel Review (PATR) report on the scheme and asked my officials to carry out further work on three scenarios recommended within it. This work is expected to conclude in Spring 2024.At present the scheme is not progressing. The Prioritisation of Major Roads Projects Programme decision by the Department in August 2023 listed York Street Interchange in Tier 3, which means development work on the scheme is paused, other than concluding the PATR work. Any future decision on its progression will be informed by the Departments emerging Transport Plans and any decision by me as Minister.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.Details of the current status of the York Street Interchange project will continue to be available on the project webpage which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/york-street-interchange-overview

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department’s funding for NI Screen in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department’s final resource and capital budget allocations to NI Screen in each of the last five years are as follows: 2023-242022-232021-222020-212019-20£’000£’000£’000£’000£’000Resource18,69017,25513,27012,99512,838Capital401,1181007575Total 18,73018,37313,37013,07012,913

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what factors, other than inflation, have contributed to the rise in the expected total cost for the Belfast Grand Central Station project.
answer_text: The full business case was approved in November 2021 at a total cost of £295m. The latest cost estimate of £340 million is £45 million higher than previously planned. The vast majority of this cost increase is due to the extraordinary inflation that has impacted on all major capital projects. However, additional costs have also been incurred for unplanned and uncosted works including poor ground conditions, additional drainage and culvert works, and additional stakeholder engagement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of Band 5 nursing vacancies in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) the number of agency staff currently covering these posts.
answer_text: Trust expenditure on agency staff has been incurred to ensure that safe and effective services are sustained and contributes importantly to maintaining service provision to patients and clients. Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts employ agency staff for several reasons, for example, cover for sickness and maternity/paternity leave; cover for existing vacancies; and when demand increases over the winter months.Information on Health and Social Care (HSC) vacancies actively being recruited by staff group and HSC Trust, is published by my Department on a quarterly basis at the following link: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/staff-vacanciesThe forthcoming publication, ‘Northern Ireland Health and Social Care Active Recruitment Statistics - Vacancies at 31 March 2024’, will be published on 22nd May 2024 and will for the first time include separate figures for Band 5 vacancies. This information is not centrally held by the Department. Transformation of Health & Social Care is a priority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department’s weed spraying programme for North Down in 2024.
answer_text: The upcoming programme for grass cutting and weed control cannot be finalised until a budget has been agreed for 2024-25.  However, it would be my intention for the Department to deliver a programme of weed control in line with previous years and in accordance with the current road maintenance standards. A copy of these can be found at:https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/infrastructure/road-maintenance-standards-for-safety-limited-service-in-2021-22-dem-160-21.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the schools in North Down he intends to include in the 20mph speed limit scheme in 2024-25.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints.  Schools have been assessed in accordance with DfI’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructureni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070The program to date has been rolled out in tranches, with the most recent completed in November 2022.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she intends to ensure that assaults on retail workers are a specific criminal offence.
answer_text: I condemn all abuse and violence directed at any frontline workers. No-one should face mistreatment whilst doing their job and serving the public and such attacks must not be tolerated. Assault of a retail worker can be prosecuted under existing laws designed to protect any member of the public from harm. However, I intend to introduce an offence of assaulting a frontline worker in my proposed Sentencing Bill and will give further consideration to protections for these workers in that context.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he intends to improve the level of respite services available for carers in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: DoH officials continue to monitor respite/short breaks across all Health and Social Care Trusts (HSCTs) via data returns to drive improvement.  The data returns consider activity across all programmes of care.  The South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust moved to a new IT system (ENCOMPASS) in November 2023.  This has temporarily impacted the Trust’s ability to provide data.  Utilising data immediately prior to ENCOMPASS demonstrates that collectively the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust is performing at 95% of the pre-COVID baseline in terms of the hours of short breaks delivered.  In relation to the total number of adult carers receiving a short break this is at 82% of the pre-COVID baseline.The Department of Health are encouraged by the amount of short break and respite provision provided by HSC Trusts.  There are issues though which can affect respite provision.   The demographic of service users has changed significantly and involves higher levels of complexity i.e. bariatric service users, nursing type care, increased need for ‘one to one’ supervision. This increased complexity impacts upon the number of people who can have their needs accommodated within the current service model. To improve access to respite/short break services, support has been offered by Trusts including the SEHSCT to service users/carers in the form of Cash Grants (one off payments), cash ‘Direct payments’, Domiciliary Short Breaks and access to nursing and residential home facilities. DoH officials are working with HSCTs to develop and progress the Shared Lives Model, to enhance access to day support and short break services. DOH officials are working with HSCTs to ensure that the current activity is being accurately reported and monitored. DOH are reviewing the metrics and baseline used to monitor remobilisation in Adult Services. Families, parents/carers and service users continue to emphasise the need for a range of day opportunities, day care, respite/short break services. Trust are now delivering this via the mechanisms noted above.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether applicants to the Basic Payment Scheme 2024 can include fields that are currently flooded in their application.
answer_text: Eligible land which is temporarily not available to a farm business on 15 May or is ineligible for Basic Payment Scheme (BPS) or any other area-based scheme at any time during the scheme year due to reasons of Force Majeure/Exceptional Circumstances (FM/EC). e.g. flooding, can be included on the Single Application.  The Department should be advised of the FM/EC event when the applicant submits their single application or, if their single application has already been submitted, within 15 working days of being in a position to do so, using Form FML1 - Force Majeure/Exceptional Circumstances.  All relevant supporting documentation should be included with the form in order that the circumstances of the event can be properly considered.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) what financial support is available for farmers who cannot use fields or have lost arable crops due to excess rain; and (ii) whether he intends to review this support.
answer_text: I am aware that this year’s very wet and late Spring is presenting challenges for farmers operating in almost every sector within the industry. Although there is no adverse weather financial support currently available for farmers, I am making representations to the UK Government about the need for central government intervention and I intend to raise this for discussion at the next meeting of the Inter-Ministerial Group for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs. Meanwhile, farmers seeking advice on management of their farming operations during this ongoing poor weather should contact their local College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise (CAFRE) Advisor for the latest technical advice and support.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what impact the testing of new equipment and software at the Hydebank Test Centre, to ensure it has been installed and commissioned in accordance with the contract specification, has had on the timescale for the opening of the test centre.
answer_text: The equipment contractor, Worldwide Environmental Products Inc, completed the installation and commissioning of new vehicle testing equipment and associated software at the Driver & Vehicle Agency’s new test centre at Hydebank in early February 2024.  The new equipment and software is currently undergoing user acceptance testing to ensure it has been installed and commissioned in accordance with the contract specification.  This equipment and software will only be signed off when I have received assurances from officials that it operates safely to the required standard.  It is anticipated Hydebank test centre will open to the public later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the proposed projects in North Down that will be part of the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme.
answer_text: My Department is developing a renewable electricity support scheme to incentivise investment, ensure a fair price for electricity produced locally and encourage diversification to support security of supply.Following our recent consultation, officials have been working with consultants to define the optimal scheme structure. The High-Level Design for the scheme was published this month as part of the Department’s response to the consultation.The next phase will involve modelling and a financial impact assessment alongside the establishment of a legislative pathway, state aid approval and institutional roles and responsibilities that are necessary for delivery.Final arrangements will be incorporated in the Detailed Design to be developed throughout the remainder of 2024. Once a scheme is launched, all eligible renewable electricity projects in the North, including North Down, will to be able to participate in the renewable electricity support scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he will protect consumers from increased energy prices as a result of the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme.
answer_text: My Department is developing a renewable electricity support scheme to incentivise investment, ensure a fair price for electricity produced locally and encourage diversification to support security of supply.Following a public consultation, officials have been working with consultants to define the optimal scheme structure. The High-Level Design for the scheme was published this month as part of the Department’s response to the consultation. A Contract for Difference scheme was agreed by the majority of respondents to the consultation to be the preferred approach to supporting renewable generation in the north. By guaranteeing a price per unit of electricity generated over the contract period, the proposed scheme can provide protection to consumers from market volatility and reduce investment risk for developers.Striking a balance is crucial in attracting sufficient projects to meet our renewable electricity targets while safeguarding consumers from potentially excessive costs. The next phase of scheme development will involve modelling and a financial impact assessment, including full consideration of the potential impact on consumers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of penalties issued for pollution incidents in North Down in each of the last five years.
answer_text: There have been no prosecutions taken forward over the last five years relating to water pollution in the North Down Constituency. One penalty is being progressed in relation to a breach of cross compliance in relation to water pollution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of planning applications that were rejected due to water capacity concerns in North Down in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Ards and North Down Borough Council is responsible for determining planning applications within the North Down area. You should therefore direct your question to that council’s planning office on (028) 9182 4006 or by e-mailing planning@ardsandnorthdown.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the introduction of the draft Strategic Framework for Ending Violence Against Women and Girls.
answer_text: We remain committed to bringing forward the Strategic Framework for Ending Violence Against Women and Girls as soon as possible.  A full public consultation was carried out in the latter half of 2023.  Following detailed analysis of the responses to the consultation a revised draft has been prepared for consideration.    Our officials are currently finalising a draft two-year Implementation Plan, with a focus on what can be delivered in the first year. The draft Framework and accompanying Action Plan is expected to be submitted for Executive consideration and approval in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) his Department’s planned funding for upgrades to the Enterprise Rail service; and (ii) whether any of the funds were reallocated from other areas of his Department’s budget.
answer_text: I welcome the announcement of the €165million investment from the PEACEPLUS Programme, that was announced earlier this month. This funding will support a major programme to transform the cross-border “Enterprise” train service, paving the way for an hourly timetable between Belfast and Dublin. This programme involves replacing the existing Enterprise trains with brand new trains by the end of this decade, allowing for an hourly timetable and faster journey times between Dublin and Belfast. The procurement process for the new train-sets is expected to be complete over the coming year, and the final costs will not therefore be determined until this statutory process is completed. The project, which is a high priority for me and my Department, will be jointly funded by the Department of Infrastructure (DfI) and the Department of Transport (DoT) as well as the confirmed PEACEPLUS allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s spending on public health promotion in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Public Health Agency (PHA) currently invests around £4.5M in Health Improvement Services annually within the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust area. These services are delivered by a range of providers including Trusts, Councils and the Community and Voluntary Sector.The services focus on improving health and wellbeing of the population and tackling health inequalities, and include services to support physical activity, mental wellbeing, suicide prevention, obesity prevention, older people and social inclusion, community based Healthy Living Centres, addictions services, support to stop smoking, early years interventions and support for parents and carers, among others.A breakdown of the funding allocated by the PHA over the past 5 years is provided in the table below: 2019/20  2020/21  2021/22  2022/23  2023/24 Total £4,356,767  £4,353542  £4,345,060  £4,578,659  £4,583,219 In addition, programmes are also funded/provided by other HSC bodies and independent sources across Northern Ireland. question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of additional enrolments available to Bangor Academy and Sixth Form College as part of the normalisation pilot scheme.
answer_text: On 22 March 2024, I announced the outcome of the Normalisation Pilot exercise, with 21 schools approved to have increases to their approved enrolment numbers.As a result, the admissions and enrolment numbers of Bangor Academy and Sixth Form College will increase to 262 and 1700 respectively. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the capital spend by his Department on MOT testing equipment at the Hydebank Test Centre.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department’s procurement process for new MOT testing equipment at the Hydebank Test Centre.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA), in collaboration with the Department of Finance’s Construction and Procurement Delivery (CPD), undertook a procurement exercise for the supply, installation, commissioning, calibration and maintenance of vehicle testing equipment, integrated test lane software, all associated IT hardware, licences and a management information system to service the DVA’s network of test centres. A pre-market engagement exercise was conducted from December 2017 to February 2018 and a tender competition was formally published via eTendersNI using the open procedure in adherence with the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 (as amended). All tenders were evaluated against the published evaluation criteria and a contract was awarded by CPD on behalf of the DVA in March 2019.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the Acute at Home Services available in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Hospital at Home provides intensive hospital level care in the community setting for patients with acute conditions that would normally require hospital admission. The care is provided by a multi professional team and the medical cover is provided by Care of the Elderly consultants and specialty doctors. In May 2023, the South Eastern Health & Social Care Trust commenced a Hospital at Home service (the new regional name for Acute Care At Home) in North Down and have now rolled this service out to the Ards locality.Within the Down and Lisburn areas, the original Enhanced Care at Home service model continues.  This service differs from Hospital at Home as the medical cover is provided by GPs and means that the service does not admit patients with the same acuity and complexity of need. The Trust plans to roll out the Hospital at Home service Trust-wide as part of wider plans to manage unscheduled care pressures.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the roll-out of multi-disciplinary teams in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The MDT model remains key to stabilising and strengthening vital Primary Care services to ensure they can continue to provide high quality care to our people, now and into the future.To date, the MDT model is fully rolled out in Down Federation and partially rolled out in North Down and Ards Federations. There is currently no MDT footprint in Lisburn.Whilst the budget for 2024/25 has not yet been set it is expected that the overall financial position for all NI departments will remain very challenging.My Department remains committed to the further implementation of the MDT model across Northern Ireland, and is developing a plan for rollout over the next number of years subject to the availability of the necessary recurrent funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action his Department is taking to promote tourist attractions in North Down, including through capital spending.
answer_text: Tourism NI (TNI) has regularly promoted the North Down Area through its seasonal marketing campaigns which are targeted to specific market segments based on extensive research. One of the most recent pieces of promotional activity was the launch of a major Spring advertising campaign on 22 January 2024 which runs until 31 March.The campaign features TV, radio, press, press supplements, outdoor, social and digital advertising, all underpinned by a comprehensive PR plan. TNI also works with its partner Tourism Ireland to host international media, with North Down featuring regularly in many itineraries.TNI has an ongoing programme of PR activity with tourism businesses and visitor experiences from North Down regularly featuring in PR content, media trips, and commissioned TV programmes on Virgin Media TV. RTE are currently filming in National Trust properties such as Mount Stewart for a primetime show in Autumn 2024. TNI also works closely with the tourism industry in North Down and across the North to support the development of engaging and immersive tourism experiences. Seven projects from the North Down constituency area have benefitted from capital funding from TNI totalling £133,520.76 since 1 April 2019.   The projects assisted included experiences such as the Pickie Fun Park, Bangor and Stand-up Paddleboarding Hub NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans his Department has to improve access to palliative care.
answer_text: I am very aware of the importance of palliative and end of life care in supporting people with palliative care needs to live well and achieve the best quality of life as their illness progresses.The majority of palliative care services are delivered by generalist health care professionals, including the patient’s GP, District Nurse and other healthcare professionals in hospitals and care homes. When individuals have more complex or unresolved needs, or symptoms which cannot be resolved by generalist teams, they would be referred to specialist palliative care, which is provided through specialist health and social care services, such as hospital-based teams and specialist palliative care community teams, including through hospices.  The Palliative Care in Partnership Programme, co-led by my Department’s Strategic Planning and Performance Group and the Public Health Agency, continues to oversee the development and improvement of palliative and end of life care for adults across Northern Ireland.  The Partnership’s remit includes:	ensuring the delivery of key priorities in palliative and end of life care both regionally and locally;	ensuring what is designed and developed is person-centred; and	advising on and sharing best practice already in place to support people with palliative care needs.The Partnership has recently agreed its workplan priorities for 2023-26.  The workplan focuses on 4 priority areas:Early identification of and timely intervention for people with palliative care needs; Co-ordination & Quality Improvement in palliative care services; Palliative care education and training; and A public health approach to palliative care. A range of workstreams have been identified and are in place to support these priority areas.  These priorities and associated workstreams will inform and support the Palliative Care in Partnership Programme and its member organisations in taking forward a range of actions to further improve palliative and end of life care for adults. In the case of palliative care for children and young people, this is provided through HSC Trusts and the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice, in line with the holistic needs of the child or young person.In terms of Paediatric Palliative Care, this has been and is an important priority for my Department.  This dates back prior to the publication of the Strategy for Children’s Palliative and End-of-Life Care, which was published in 2016.  This strategy sets out 23 objectives across the spectrum of paediatric palliative care from diagnosis through to bereavement care.  Thankfully, as a result of innovations in treatment, children born or who develop palliative care conditions survive much longer, many into adulthood.  Whilst this is welcomed, it highlights the importance of the objectives within the Strategy and how crucial it is that those in the system work closely together to improve care and outcomes for children and young people in Northern Ireland.Through funding from my De

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the upgrade of the Kinnegar Wastewater Treatment Works, Holywood.
answer_text: The query you have raised is an operational matter for NI Water. You should therefore direct your question to NI Water through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department’s plans for land owned by Invest NI in North Down.
answer_text: The Invest NI owned land in North Down is Balloo South Industrial Estate. Following on from the Independent Review of Invest NI, the organisation has recently sought the views of all Councils, including Ards and North Down, over the future development of industrial property with a particular focus on opportunities that might present for areas of co-operation.   These responses will be essential in shaping a new industrial land and property strategy which Invest NI intends to publish later this year.   In the meantime, the organisation continues to market its land to potential investors seeking business investment locations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how his Department intends to provide MOT appointments for those whose car tax is due to expire.
answer_text: The latest information on MOTs is available on NI Direct at A guide to booking your MOT/ vehicle test | nidirect and is also included as an advice note with every MOT reminder letter issued to customers. The Driver & Vehicle Agency’s (DVA) advice to vehicle owners who require an MOT to tax their vehicle and cannot secure a date before their MOT expires is to:book the earliest available appointment; keep checking the booking system for an appointment before their MOT expires. If a customer gets to within 10 days of the expiry date of their vehicle tax and they have been unable to secure an appointment, or their vehicle tax has expired, they should complete the short notice appointment request form, and the DVA will do their best to get an urgent appointment for the vehicle.  To date, all customers who have contacted the DVA in these circumstances have been offered an earlier appointment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on paternity leave provided to male teachers.
answer_text: ANSWERTeachers’ terms & conditions of employment, including Paternity Leave are agreed at the forum known as Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which includes representatives from teachers’ employers; sectoral interests, Department of Education and the five main teachers’ unions. All teachers may also avail of Shared Parental Leave.Paternity leave policy for male teachers can be found : TNC 2015/3 - Teachers' Paternity Leave SchemeShared Parental Leave policy for all teachers can be found :TNC 2015/5 - Teachers' Shared Parental Leave Scheme

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether waiting time data will be immediately accurate and validated when the Encompass system is rolled out across the remaining Health and Social Care Trusts.
answer_text: The encompass programme will work with each Health and Social Care Trust as they roll out with encompass. They will actively monitor and validate waiting time information against baselines supplied and confirmed by each individual Trust. Further assurance of the accuracy of this information will be provided through testing conducted by each Trust prior to the system going live.

answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust provides CAMHS Eating Disorder Services for children and young people residing in both Belfast and South Eastern HSC Trust areas. Referrals for young people residing in South Eastern HSC Trust areas the last 5 years are set out in table 1.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) his Department’s criteria for providing a new salt grit box; and (ii) whether he plans to review the criteria.
answer_text: My Department currently commits significant resources to maintaining approximately 5,600 salt bins that are provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis, on roads which do not qualify for inclusion on the gritting schedule. The criteria for providing new salt bins can be found at:https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/winter-service-rsppg-e022Salt bins can also be provided to schools that are regularly affected by severe wintry weather.I hope that you appreciate that it is simply not possible to fulfil all requests for salt bins which is why my Department applies a set of criteria to the assessment. The most effective use of our limited resource is to provide salt bins on those roads were traffic volumes and other features such as gradient mean that winter impacts are the greatest, and so this forms the basis of the current policy. In these circumstances, I currently have no plans to review the criteria used for the provision of salt bins.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the waiting time figures for the Emergency Department at the Ulster Hospital from 9 November 2023 onwards will be available.
answer_text: Production of official information such as waiting times follows a strict protocol to ensure they are reliable and accurate. Delays in producing statistics following the deployment of the encompass system in the South Eastern Trust was predicted due to the need to audit the information reported by the system to ensure it presents the complete picture. This work is currently ongoing. Regular reporting of statistics will resume once validation checks are complete and the data has been assured as reliable. This will include retrospective collation of data from the Go Live date of 9 November 2023.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on biennial MOT testing for cars, light goods vehicles and motorcycles.
answer_text: I have agreed to explore with my officials the potential for biennial vehicle testing in the North. You may wish to note that a move to biennial MOT testing would require the development of primary legislation and would be subject to public consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the future of the concessionary fare scheme.
answer_text: The consultation on the NI Concessionary Fares Scheme ran for a period of 12 weeks from 1st June 2023 to 24th August 2023 and received over 20,000 responses.I am currently considering the outcome of the consultation and will provide an update in due course. My intention is to retain the services as they are, however, any decisions taken in regard to the future of the Scheme will be dependent on the budget made available to my Department and may require the support of my Executive colleagues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the sale of buildings in the Northern Ireland Civil Service estate.
answer_text: From 2019/20 to 2020/21 my department sold 4 buildings. A Review of the Department of Finance Office Estate was subsequently agreed in 2022 and will further rationalise the estate with the aim of reducing its in-scope footprint by 40% by March 2028. The reduction in office space includes exiting leased accommodation, the sale of owned offices and the transfer of assets within the public sector.In the current, 2023/24 financial year, 1 building has been sold with 2 sales to public or third sector organisations progressing which will complete after 1 April 2024. A further 3 buildings are currently on the market with completion projected for the 2024/25 financial year. My Department plans to sell a further 8 buildings by March 2028, the end of the first phase of the rationalisation programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department’s capital spending on tourism in North Down, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Tourism NI works closely with the tourism industry in North Down and across Northern Ireland to support the development of engaging and immersive tourism experiences. Seven projects from the North Down constituency area have benefitted from capital funding received from Tourism NI totalling £133,520.76 since 1 April 2019. Of the grant offered, £55,880 was offered in 2020-21, £39,007.36 was offered in 2021-22 with £38,633.40 offered in the 2022-23 financial year. The projects assisted were across a variety of funding programmes including Website Development and Experience Development Programmes and including experiences such as Pickie Fun Park, Bangor and Stand-up Paddleboarding (SUP) Hub NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the consultation for changes to the Concessionary Fares Scheme.
answer_text: The 12-week public consultation on changes to eligibility for the NI Concessionary Fares Scheme closed on 24th August 2023 and I am currently considering the responses.While I am keen to retain the Scheme as it is, this will depend on the funding available to my Department and I may need the support of my Executive colleagues to continue to provide the Scheme in its current format.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's spending on agency staff in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Trust expenditure on agency staff is necessary to ensure that safe and effective services are sustained.  The South Eastern Trust, like other HSC Trusts, employs locum staff for several reasons such as maternity/paternity leave, sickness leave, cover for existing vacancies, increased demand on services etc.The costs for agency staff have increased in recent years resulting in significant financial pressures for the Trust at a time of increasing budgetary constraints.  Working with Trusts, The Department has acted to try to alleviate the workforce pressures across the HSC including agency/locum expenditure.  The work of the Regional Agency Reduction Implementation Group has made significant progress towards the cessation of ‘off framework’ agency use in Nursing and Midwifery and Social Workers across the HSC.    All use of agency for Social Workers across the whole of the HSC ceased at the end of June 2023, with a few exceptional circumstances. There has also been a focus on recruitment, retention, and training of staff, in addition to a dedicated workstream examining the reform of the bank system.  The overall net reduction on agency spend is projected. on a straight-line basis, across all Trusts, to be in the region of £20m.  It should be noted these figures are estimates and should be treated with caution at this time.Transformation of health and social care in Northern Ireland is a priority for all in the HSC.The South Eastern HSC Trust is forecast to spend £43,768,000 on agency staff across all professional groups this year.  This compares to £24,491,000 in 2018/19.  Since 2018/19, the Trust has spent a total of £214,299,000 on agency staff as shown below.Year2018/192019/202020/212021/222022/232023/24*Total£00024,49126,75136,12439,38943,77643,768214,299*Spend up to 31st January 2024 projected for full year

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the role of surgical hubs in reducing waiting times for surgery.
answer_text: Dedicated elective care centres are designed to provide a resource for less complex planned surgery/procedures and are a means to increase productivity, efficiency, and reliability of the service. By providing services for the region, they also aim to ensure that patients have equitable access to the care they need, irrespective of where they live.  Dedicated capacity for elective surgery has been enhanced through continued development of elective care centres across Northern Ireland.  We have Day Procedure Centres operational at Lagan Valley Hospital and Omagh Hospital supporting a range of surgical specialties.  We have three Elective Overnight Stay Centres at the Mater Hospital, South West Acute Hospital and Daisy Hill Hospital for intermediate complexity surgery that may require an overnight stay in hospital.  In addition, there are cataracts centres at Downe, South Tyrone and Mid Ulster Hospitals and an orthopaedic hub at Musgrave Park Hospital which includes the Duke of Connaught Unit, a dedicated orthopaedic Day Procedure Centre.As a result, positive improvements have been shown.  At December 2023, inpatient or day case treatment waiting lists have seen promising improvements. Whilst still at unacceptable levels, there has been an 12.7% (14,259) decrease in numbers waiting compared to the same month the previous year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what measures his Department is taking to protect poultry farmers from Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI) subtype H5N1.
answer_text: My department regularly issues communications to local industry to heighten awareness and deliver key messages on how to protect the Northern Ireland (NI) poultry flock from an Avian Influenza (AI) incursion.  Veterinary Risk Assessments on the likelihood of an AI incursion are regularly reviewed with the most recent being completed in January 2024.  This is available to view on the DAERA website.  A communications campaign across various social media channels emphasising the importance of good biosecurity is ongoing.  A biosecurity self-assessment tool is available on the DAERA website, along with guidance to assist all bird keepers to review, maintain and enhance robust biosecurity measures to protect their birds against the threat of AI.In July 2023, my Department launched the ‘DAERA Dead Wild Bird Online Reporting Tool’ where members of the public can upload instantly details of dead wild birds.  This surveillance of AI is to understand if and when the virus is present in NI. Officials will continue to monitor the situation across these islands to determine the risk levels to the NI poultry flock and will continue to engage with industry, and colleagues in both Great Britain and the Republic of Ireland, to mitigate the risk of an incursion and minimise any potential impact on the poultry industry.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s expenditure on the installation and roll-out of the Encompass IT system to date.
answer_text: To date, committed spend equates to £91.4m.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance what action her Department is taking to reduce losses due to unpaid domestic and non-domestic rates.
answer_text: Land & Property Services (LPS) has robust billing, collection and recovery processes in place to maximise rate collection and reduce rating debt.  These processes include issuing annual rate bills and final demands for payment.  Where payment or arrangements to pay are not made, LPS will initiate recovery procedures, this includes formal written correspondence, e-mails and direct telephone contact with ratepayers.LPS will progress legal recovery actions for persistent non-payment. This includes progression through the Magistrate Courts, the Enforcement of Judgement’s Office and initiation of bankruptcy proceeding. Ratepayers experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact LPS to discuss payment options which can include restructuring current payment plans or establishing longer term payment arrangements to enable payment of rate arrears.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much of the DfI Roads Southern Division budget for resurfacing roads has been spent in North Down in the last three financial years.
answer_text: My Department does not maintain records of budget in the format requested, however details of Capital Structural Maintenance expenditure is provided for Ards & North Down Borough Council area (see Table 1 below).Table 1: Capital Structural Maintenance  £’mCapital Structural Maintenance Expenditure 20-21 21-2222-23Ards & North Down Borough Council4.55.42.3Capital Structural Maintenance covers resurfacing and reconstruction, asphalt patching, surface dressing, capital investment in both drainage infrastructure and in the structural stability of embankments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what discussions she has had with the Chief Constable in relation to plans to (i) strengthen; and (ii) increase the presence of neighbourhood policing teams.
answer_text: I meet with the Chief Constable on a regular basis, and we discuss a range of issues affecting policing in Northern Ireland. However, decisions relating to the resourcing and deployment of Neighbourhood Policing Teams are an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. I am committed to respecting his operational independence and the role of the Board in holding him to account. You may, therefore, wish to direct your question to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the cost of food waste, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The monitoring of food waste and the methodology used to calculate costs varies significantly across HSCT Trust areas. The figures provided below cannot be used as a comparator between Trust area and further analysis would require significant work to provide costs using a consistent methodology. No Trust was able to provide figures before 2019/20.Table 1:Year2019/20 (£,000)2020/21(£,000)2021/22(£,000)2022/23(£,000)2023/24(£,000)BHSCTN/P406497643N/PNHSCT16141719N/PSEHSCT2724252929SHSCT25262224N/PWHSCTN/PN/PN/PN/PN/P*N/P – Not ProvidedNotes from each Trust on the figures provided in Table 1 above:BHSCTFigures provided are for the Royal Group of Hospitals, Mater, Musgrave Park and Belfast City Hospital sites and does not include Community services.Figures are collected using food waste data, which is collected on each site.  In order to provide a cost at these figures, Hotel/Support Services staff have used a crude average wastage percentage of 20% for patient feeding.To calculate a cost, the yearly food costs identified on each service have been multiplied with the associated wastage percentage.  Therefore the increase on wastage costs year on year also reflects the increased cost of food and beverage.NHSCTThe spend provided above relates to the collection and the processing of food waste either to compost it or anaerobically digest it to produce green energy.SETNo further information was provided by the Trust on the methodology used to calculate the food waste treatment costs provided. However, the figure provided for 2023/24 is based on a full year forecast based on 10mth actual costs.SHSCTNo further information was provided by the Trust on the methodology used to calculate the food waste treatment costs provided.WHSCTThe monitoring of food wastage was largely stood down and it is not possible to provide figures over the last 5 years. The Trust is currently considering reinstatement of monitoring arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the roll-out of the Encompass system across all Health and Social Care Trusts.
answer_text: Encompass was successfully deployed in the South Eastern Trust on 9th November 2023. It was the first Trust in Northern Ireland to deploy the solution with over 12,000 staff trained and accessing the system.  Preparation is well underway with the remaining Trust deployments.  Belfast Trust is set to Go-Live on 6th June, Northern Trust scheduled for Autumn 2024, and Southern and Western Trusts scheduled for Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on funding for new build projects at (i) Priory Integrated College; and (ii) Bangor Central Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: I have placed the 10 remaining former Fresh Start projects that are not yet in contract into my Department’s conventional major works programme to continue to advance in design and planning.  The projects will proceed to construction when planning is complete and affordable within the wider education budget context.All of the projects have been submitted as capital bids for 2024-25 to the Department of Finance and I trust all Executive parties will support these bids for capital funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail all the support his Department can provide to unpaid female carers who are leaving their workplace due to a lack of support.
answer_text: A high level of economic inactivity has been a persistent issue for our economy for many years with rates particularly high for those with disabilities, women and those from disadvantaged areas.  I am aware that caring responsibilities are a key issue for women in particular.  I am committed to working with the Department of Education on the development and implementation of the Early Years and Childcare Strategy.Following a co-design and consultation process, it is my intention to bring forward an employment rights Bill within the current Assembly mandate to ensure the employment law framework remains fit for purpose and operates effectively for both businesses and workers. Carer’s Leave is an issue that I will be happy to consider in that context.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans his Department has to simplify the process for GB driving licence holders living in Northern Ireland to apply for a practical driving test.
answer_text: In order to take a practical driving test in the North, it is a legal requirement that the candidate must be normally a resident, which means that a person lives here for at least 185 days in each calendar year.  Usually, learner drivers demonstrate this as part of the process of obtaining a provisional driving licence here.  Once a provisional driving licence has been issued, the candidate can proceed to book their practical test online. Learner drivers who hold a GB provisional driving licence are asked to demonstrate residency here when they are booking their practical driving test, through successful completion of a residency check.  This process is in place to ensure compliance with the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (NI) 1996 and the Road Traffic (NI) Order 1981, both as amended.Alternatively, learner drivers holding a GB provisional driving licence who are resident here can apply to the Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) for a provisional driving licence, and then proceed to book their theory or practical test online. There are currently no plans to change the process for GB provisional driving licence holders living in the North to apply for a practical driving test.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans his Department has to help high street hospitality businesses.
answer_text: Tourism NI, Failte Ireland and Tourism Ireland are working in partnership to deliver a €7.6m programme to improve synergies and experiences for visitors to the Causeway Coastal Route (CCR) and Wild Atlantic Way (WAW).This programme will deliver increased connectivity and improved visitor experience across the WAW and the CCR, providing an opportunity to bring together two of the most internationally renowned experiences that the island of Ireland has to offer. By demonstrating to the international visitor, the connectivity of the two routes and showcasing the Northwest of the island as a gateway to these two iconic experiences, it will create a dynamic proposition encouraging international visitors to explore more widely, stay longer, and spend more.One strand of the programme will be to undertake an audit of the CCR to assess the current visitor experience and develop appropriate discovery points along the route in line with the current WAW experience. This will include concepting of signage along the route. This work will be carried out in 2024 and all key stakeholders will be engaged before recommendations are implemented. A second strand of the programme aims to launch a small capital grants scheme for public and private businesses on both sides of the border. This will allow businesses aligned with the brands to apply for grant funding to improve interpretation, visitor experience etc. The scheme is being developed in partnership with the three agencies with the aim to launch by summer 2024.A programme of consumer research is currently live in four overseas market (Britain, USA, France & Germany). Findings from the research will inform a market campaign to be launched later in 2024 in one overseas market.
